title: Clinic intervention example dataset — clinic
text: small synthetic mental-health wellbeing intervention sample, used package's messy-data companion community. community clean default, clinic deliberately carries undeclared missing-value codes, column whose value labels stripped import, imperfect scale item, declare--clean workflow realistic material work . 70 clients 16 variables echo teaching structure community – interaction, null variable, non-overlapping missingness, recode dichotomy, clean logistic outcome, multi-category variable, Likert battery – psychology setting. data synthetic, relationships among variables realistic.

title: Clinic intervention example dataset — clinic
Clinic intervention example dataset — clinic
text: data frame 70 rows 16 variables: ClientID Client ID, character ("C001", "C002", ...). Stress Perceived stress (integer, 0-40). Carries SPSS-style missing values (-99 Refused, -98 know). SocialSupport Perceived social support (integer, 0-24); buffering partner Stress--SocialSupport interaction Flourishing. SleepHours Average nightly sleep hours. Carries SPSS-style missing values (-99 Refused, -98 know), placed cases overlap Stress codes, model using predictors drops cases either alone. Flourishing Flourishing score (integer, 0-100); built Stress--SocialSupport interaction (buffering hypothesis). ScreenTime Daily screen time hours; deliberately near-independent variables. PriorTherapy Received therapy study, dichotomy coded 1/2 (1 Yes, 2 ); recode 0/1 use logistic-regression outcome. SoughtHelp Sought professional help study, dichotomy coded 0/1 (0 , 1 Yes). clean logistic-regression outcome. Medication Currently taking medication, dichotomy coded 0/1 (0 , 1 Yes). Carries SPSS-style missing value (-99 Refused). Condition Treatment condition, 4 categories (1 Control, 2 CBT, 3 Mindfulness, 4 Support group); modest effect Flourishing. MoodRating Mood rating (integer, 1-10). Arrives "dirty": literal -99 (Refused) -98 (know) codes present data missing-value declaration, state play CSV Excel import. package's jdeclare_udm() demonstration variable: summary statistics poisoned codes declared. Anxiety1 "felt calm relaxed." 5-point Likert (1 5 Extremely); reverse-keyed (variable label ends " R"). Reverse-code scale scoring. Anxiety2 "worried many different things." 5-point Likert. Arrives literal -99/-98 codes present data missing-value declaration – undeclared contrast Anxiety4. Anxiety3 "felt afraid clear reason." 5-point Likert; arrives value labels stripped (plain numeric column, CSV import dropped labels). Anxiety4 "trouble controlling worry." 5-point Likert. Carries properly declared SPSS-style missing values (-99 Refused, -98 know) – declared contrast Anxiety2. Anxiety5 "felt restless edge." 5-point Likert; weakly loaded (Cronbach's-alpha drop candidate scoring scale).

title: Clinic intervention example dataset — clinic
text: Synthetic data generated data-raw/clinic_data_generator.R (random seed 20260614).

title: Clinic intervention example dataset — clinic
text: five Anxiety items form single scale, one deliberate problem per item: Anxiety1 reverse-keyed; Anxiety2 carries literal -99/-98 codes declared missing; Anxiety3 arrives value labels stripped; Anxiety4 carries -99/-98 codes properly declared (clean contrast Anxiety2); Anxiety5 weak item scale-reliability output flags dropping. Stress SleepHours carry SPSS-style missing values non-overlapping cases, listwise deletion across reduces analysis sample per-variable counts. MoodRating Anxiety2 two columns whose -99/-98 codes arrive undeclared, awaiting jdeclare_udm(). Stress--SocialSupport interaction Flourishing buffering hypothesis (higher social support weakens negative association stress flourishing), treatment Condition modest effect Flourishing.

title: Community survey example dataset — community
text: small synthetic survey dataset used throughout package runnable example. backs function examples, serves teaching dataset new users, demonstrates cross-platform save load behavior. 100 respondents 15 variables chosen exercise kinds data social-science users actually : Likert scales, dichotomies, multi-category variable, continuous measures, SPSS-style user-defined missing values. data synthetic, relationships among variables realistic.

title: Community survey example dataset — community
Community survey example dataset — community
text: data frame 100 rows 15 variables: RespondentID Respondent ID, character ("R001", "R002", ...). Income Annual income (USD). Carries SPSS-style missing values (-99 Refused, -98 know). Education Highest education level, 5 categories (1 high school, 2 High school graduate, 3 college, 4 Bachelor's degree, 5 Graduate degree). Carries SPSS-style missing values (-99 Refused, -98 know). Age Age years (integer, 18-80). WellbeingScore Flourishing score (integer, 0-100); built Income--Age interaction. Volunteer Volunteered past year, dichotomy coded 0/1 (0 , 1 Yes). OwnsHome Owns home, dichotomy coded 1/2 (1 Yes, 2 ); recode 0/1 use logistic-regression outcome. Smoker Current smoker, dichotomy coded 0/1 (0 , 1 Yes). Carries SPSS-style missing value (-99 Refused). CommuteTime Daily commute time minutes (integer); deliberately near-independent variables. Region Region residence, 4 categories (1 North, 2 South, 3 East, 4 West). Environment1 "Climate change serious threat." 5-point Likert (1 Strongly Disagree 5 Strongly Agree). Carries SPSS-style missing values (-99 Refused, -98 know). Environment2 "Concern environment exaggerated." 5-point Likert; reverse-keyed (variable label ends " R"). Reverse-code scale scoring. Environment3 "Government environment." 5-point Likert. Carries SPSS-style missing values (-99 Refused, -98 know). Environment4 "pay environmentally friendly products." 5-point Likert. Environment5 "Pollution major cause public health problems." 5-point Likert; weakly loaded (Cronbach's-alpha drop candidate scoring scale).

title: Community survey example dataset — community

title: Community survey example dataset — community
text: community clean default example dataset. companion dataset deliberately carries undeclared missing-value codes, stripped value labels, imperfect scale – material data-cleaning workflow operates – see clinic. five Environment items form single attitude scale: item 2 reverse-keyed, item 5 weak item scale-reliability output flags dropping. Income, Education, Smoker, Environment1, Environment3 carry SPSS-style missing values, codes placed partly non-overlapping cases listwise deletion visibly reduces analysis sample per-variable counts. community's missing-value codes properly declared; dataset undeclared codes deliberate data-cleaning problems, see clinic.

what: Internal helper: apply a logical mask expression to a data frame — .jst_apply_mask
title: Internal helper: apply a logical mask expression to a data frame — .jst_apply_mask
text: Shared mechanic Step 2 (persistent jsubset) Step 3 (per-call subset = argument) .jst_apply_pipeline(). Evaluates expr data + caller environment, coerces NAs resulting mask FALSE, returns filtered data frame. two callers differ upstream source (joptions state vs. argument) downstream bookkeeping (sample_info slot populated); masking step identical.

title: Internal helper: apply a logical mask expression to a data frame — .jst_apply_mask
Internal helper: apply a logical mask expression to a data frame — .jst_apply_mask
text: data Data frame mask. expr Unevaluated logical expression (language object). envir Environment evaluate expr . Data columns take precedence; envir provides fallback bindings. on_error One "warn" "stop". "warn" emits warning returns data unchanged – used persistent jsubset state, expression validated set runtime failure unexpected. "stop" raises error – used per-call subset = argument, broken expression user error call time. stage_label Character. Prefix used error/warning message (e.g. "jsubset" "Subset") failures attributable right pipeline stage.

title: Internal helper: apply a logical mask expression to a data frame — .jst_apply_mask

what: Internal helper: class-safe numeric coercion for haven-input columns — .jst_as_numeric
title: Internal helper: class-safe numeric coercion for haven-input columns — .jst_as_numeric
text: Equivalent .numeric(x) every input type (numeric, factor, Date/POSIXct/difftime, character, haven_labelled give result, since unclass() strips class attribute), bypasses vctrs method dispatch. bare .numeric() haven_labelled vector can abort "convert <haven_labelled> " fresh session readxl attached haven registered vec_cast method (always aborts character-backed haven_labelled). Stripping class first sidesteps dispatch entirely. Standardised package-wide haven-input coercion sites jdesc, jfreq, jscreen, jt, jaov, jcrosstab, jcorr, jlm, jlogistic, jalpha, jdummy, jrecode. (Session 50)

title: Internal helper: flag a registered classification that fights the data — .jst_declaration_plausibility
text: plausibility envelope per role: "count" – non-negative whole numbers two distinct values. Flagged negative value, non-whole value, exactly two distinct values (reads dichotomy). "likert" – non-negative whole numbers within 0 10 11 distinct points. Flagged value falls outside range, value non-whole, 11 distinct values. "dummy" – 11 categories, flagged high end (11 categories). lower floor: two-category (dichotomy) dummy never flagged. "numeric" plausibility target – declaring variable Numeric maximally permissive assertion – returns "". Declared-missing codes removed structure judged (central .jst_missing_info() reader, SPSS-style na_values / na_range Stata-/SAS-style tagged NAs handled). Likert item carrying --range missing sentinel (e.g. 99 = "Refused") therefore judged real scale points, flagged sentinel. count Likert checks read numeric codes; dummy category count taken surviving values type preserved, character/factor identifier counted distinct labels.

text: x variable / data-frame column. var_name Optional character string naming variable; required (data_name) consult registered intent. data_name Optional character string naming data frame; required (var_name) consult registered intent. override Optional per-call asserted role ("numeric", "categorical", "count"); highest-priority tier supplied.

title: Internal helper: jstats analysis-role class for display — .jst_jstats_class
text: list class (character), subclass (character, "" none), source (one "per-call", "registered", "measure", "structural").

title: Internal helper: jstats analysis-role class for display — .jst_jstats_class
text: Class (analysis role): one "Numeric", "Categorical", "Numbers--text", "Date-time", "Unsupported". Storage facts (labelled vs plain, character backing) live jscreen's separate "Base R Type" column, never – base-R numeric can resolve Numeric, Categorical (dichotomy), Categorical (N-category), depending analysis-relevant structure. Sub-class (Categorical ; "" otherwise): "dichotomy" two- value variable, "Likert" value-labelled ordered scale (consecutive run 3-7 surviving labelled codes plus anchor--battery discriminator; structural detection jlikert() assertion), "identifier" text/factor variable whose every non- missing value distinct (7+ values; respondent ID typical case), else "N-category" (e.g. "4-category") count distinct non-missing values. "Likert" "identifier" labels display refinements: variable still Categorical every analysis screening purpose. boundary Numeric Categorical exactly package's existing rule: dichotomy (coding), factor / logical / character, haven-labelled variable <= 6 categories, whole-number 0-6 numeric Categorical; everything else numeric-ish (continuous numeric, labelled 7+ categories) Numeric. Numeric subclass "Count" registration-(set via jcount, per-call override "count"); structural classifier never emits . Categorical subclass "-cat dummy" (e.g. "5-cat dummy") likewise registration-– set via jdummy() variable two categories; structural classifier never emits , dichotomy declared via jdummy() keeps "dichotomy" subclass, dichotomy special case dummy. Resolution stack (highest wins; first tier yields class short- circuits). Storage-determined edge kinds (date-time, numbers--text, unsupported) resolve structurally front role-assertion targets, user tiers operate among Numeric, Categorical, Count: (1) per-call override -> source "per-call"; (2) registered intent – .jst_registry notebook (jnumeric/jcount) .jst_dummy registry (jdummy -> categorical) -> source "registered"; (3) SPSS measure – designed UNPOPULATED v1, ignored; (4) structural guess -> source "structural". Identity (var_name + data_name) required consult tiers 1-2; omitted, resolver returns structural answer source "structural", bare .jst_jstats_class(x) behaves now also reports source.

text: x vector – haven_labelled, factor, character, numeric. var_name Character. variable's name (used dummy column prefix). ref Reference category specifier. May first (default), last, numeric code, character string matching canonical label. name.length.warn Integer. Warn final dummy name exceeds many characters. Default 30. max.categories Integer. title: Internal helper: build canonical dummy variable naming for a categorical variable — .jst_make_dummy_names
text: Supports six input shapes: haven_labelled descriptive labels containing variable name (e.g. Gender labelled "Male", "Female"). haven_labelled descriptive labels already containing variable name (e.g. Program labelled "Program 1", "Program 2"...). haven_labelled labels equal codes strings (.e. uninformative – labels carry extra information). Plain numeric labels. Factor character levels. Character vector. Naming algorithm: Output form always VarName_Suffix. Suffix source per category: descriptive label available, numeric code otherwise. Mixed within single variable allowed (descriptive wins per-category). Canonicalise chosen suffix: replace runs non-alphanumeric characters single underscore; trim leading trailing underscores; suffix canonicalises empty (label entirely non-alphanumeric), fall back category's code. Anti-stutter: canonicalised suffix already begins paste0(var_name, "_"), prepend variable name . Detect duplicates: two categories produce final name, stop error pointing jrelabel(). Permissive reference matching: ref character string, three matching attempts made – direct match canonical labels, canonicalised user input matched canonical labels ("Program 3" "3" find "Program_3"), string match codes ("3" also matches code 3).

text: df data frame print. col.names Optional character vector column headers. NULL, uses names(df). row.names Logical. TRUE, includes row names first column. align Optional character vector alignment codes ("l", "r", "c", "d"), one per displayed column. NULL, auto-detects: numeric = right, character/= left. Code "d" decimal-tab: data cells right-justified (uniform decimal-places column aligns decimal point) header stays centered column. caption Optional title string printed table. indent Number leading spaces data row. Default 0, data rows sit flush column 1, aligned caption, header, separator (use header.indent). Callers want nested/indented sub-table pass positive value (e.g. indent = 4). header.indent Number leading spaces caption, header row, separator row. Defaults 0. default indent, header data share left edge; raise one relative special layouts.

what: One-way ANOVA (traditional or Welch method) — jaov
title: One-way ANOVA (traditional or Welch method) — jaov
text: Runs one-way ANOVA prints formatted group descriptives table followed ANOVA table. default, runs traditional ANOVA assuming equal variances. Optional parameters provide post-hoc tests, effect size, Levene's test, confidence intervals. Set welch = TRUE Welch correction equal variances assumed. Handles haven-labelled, numeric, factor grouping variables. haven-labelled variables, numeric codes displayed alongside labels group descriptives table.

title: One-way ANOVA (traditional or Welch method) — jaov
code: jaov(   formula,   data,   welch = FALSE,   posthoc = NULL,   effect.size = NULL,   levene = NULL,   ci = NULL,   subset = NULL,   variable.id = NULL,   value.id = NULL,   case.processing.detail = NULL,   full = FALSE,   digits = NULL )

title: One-way ANOVA (traditional or Welch method) — jaov
text: formula formula form DV ~ Group. data data frame containing variables referenced formula. welch Logical. FALSE (default), runs traditional ANOVA. TRUE, runs Welch's ANOVA (assume equal variances). posthoc Logical NULL. TRUE, prints Tukey HSD pairwise comparisons. available welch = TRUE. NULL (default), defers joutput(). effect.size Logical NULL. TRUE, prints eta-squared. NULL (default), defers joutput(). levene Logical NULL. TRUE, prints Levene's test homogeneity variance. NULL (default), defers joutput(). ci Logical NULL. TRUE, adds 95% confidence intervals group descriptives table. NULL (default), defers joutput(). subset optional unquoted logical expression (e.g. Group == 1) subset cases call . Applied jcomplete jsubset. affect function calls. variable.id Character NULL. Variable label display mode: one "", "names", "labels", "legend", "legend.bottom". "names" shows variable names ; "" shows "name: label"; "labels" shows DV grouping-variable labels wherever variable name appears (table captions ANOVA Source row; group levels follow value.id mode) – best short labels; "legend"/"legend.bottom" keep names print label legend output. NULL (default) defers joutput()'s variable.id setting. logical. value.id Character NULL. Value-label display mode group descriptives rows: "" ("code: label"), "values" (bare code), "labels" (label, degrading bare code code none). "legend" "legend.bottom" keep bare code table print value-label legend ("legend" per-table, "legend.bottom" consolidated multiple tables produced). -op grouping variables value labels. NULL (default) defers joutput()'s value.id setting. logical. case.processing.detail Per-call override Case Processing Summary detail tier: one "none", "totals", "per_code". NULL (default) uses active joutput() level default. full Logical. TRUE, turns posthoc, effect.size, levene, ci . override explicit FALSE values. digits Integer NULL. Number decimal places continuous statistics output tables (range 0-7; digits = 0 prints whole numbers trailing decimal point). affect p-values, percentages, integer quantities (counts, N, degrees freedom), keep fixed conventions. NULL (default) defers joutput()'s digits setting (default 3).

title: One-way ANOVA (traditional or Welch method) — jaov
text: Invisibly returns list class jst_anova containing: model (aov oneway.test object), model_frame (analysis data frame used plotting), test_type, formula, descriptives, f, df1, df2, p, eta_squared, n, sample_info (pipeline missing data counts).

title: One-way ANOVA (traditional or Welch method) — jaov
text: red title identifying test type printed first, followed variable labels (present), results tables.

what: Compute a row-wise mean across multiple variables — javg
title: Compute a row-wise mean across multiple variables — javg
text: javg() computes mean values across multiple variables case (row) data frame. typically used create scale means set related items. default, cases missing values receive NA. Use min.valid argument allow partial means — example, min.valid = 1 computes mean available values long least one item non-missing. default, denominator number non-missing values case. Use fixed = TRUE always divide total number variables regardless missing values. Variables can listed individually using colon notation select range consecutive columns (e.g. Attitude1:Attitude6).

title: Compute a row-wise mean across multiple variables — javg
code: javg(data, ..., min.valid = NULL, fixed = FALSE, var.label = NULL)

title: Compute a row-wise mean across multiple variables — javg
text: data data frame, omit use juse() default. ... Unquoted variable names. Use colon notation (e.g. Attitude1:Attitude6) select range consecutive columns. min.valid Integer (optional). minimum number non-missing values required compute mean. case fewer non-missing values, result NA. omitted, values must non-missing (equivalent setting min.valid number variables). fixed Logical. FALSE (default), denominator case number non-missing values (.e. mean adjusts missing data). TRUE, denominator always total number variables (.e. missing values effectively count zero). var.label Character string (optional). variable label attach result. omitted, auto-generated label used.

title: Compute a row-wise mean across multiple variables — javg
text: numeric vector length nrow(data), suitable assigning new column: MyData$ScaleMean <- javg(Var1, Var2, Var3).

title: Compute a row-wise mean across multiple variables — javg
code: # Set the default data frame (so you can omit it in function calls) juse(community) #> Default data frame set to: community  # Mean of three variables (all must be non-missing) community$EnvAvg <- javg(Environment1, Environment3, Environment4) #> Mean of 3 variables computed for 100 cases (18 set to NA due to missing values). #> Mean of the new variable: 3.073. #>  #> Note: javg() returns the scores; assign them to a column to keep them: #>   community$<name> <- javg(...) #> For the full distribution (min, max, SD), run jdesc() on the new column.  # Mean with partial data allowed (at least 2 non-missing) community$EnvAvg <- javg(Environment1, Environment3, Environment4,                          min.valid = 2) #> Mean of 3 variables computed for 100 cases (min.valid = 2: 12 cases used partial data, 6 set to NA due to missing values). #> Mean of the new variable: 3.112. #>  #> Note: javg() returns the scores; assign them to a column to keep them: #>   community$<name> <- javg(...) #> For the full distribution (min, max, SD), run jdesc() on the new column.  # Mean using colon range for consecutive columns community$ScaleMean <- javg(Environment1:Environment5) #> Mean of 5 variables computed for 100 cases (18 set to NA due to missing values). #> Mean of the new variable: 2.995. #>  #> Note: javg() returns the scores; assign them to a column to keep them: #>   community$<name> <- javg(...) #> For the full distribution (min, max, SD), run jdesc() on the new column.  # Mix colon ranges and explicit names (e.g. after reverse-coding an item) community$Environment2R <- jrecode(community, Environment2,                                    map = "1=5; 2=4; 3=3; 4=2; 5=1") #>  #> Note: jrecode() returns the recoded values; assign them to a column to keep them: #>   community$<name> <- jrecode(...) #> To check the recode landed correctly, compare jfreq() on the original and the new column. community$ScaleMean <- javg(Environment1, Environment2R,                             Environment3:Environment5) #> Mean of 5 variables computed for 100 cases (18 set to NA due to missing values). #> Mean of the new variable: 3.083. #>  #> Note: javg() returns the scores; assign them to a column to keep them: #>   community$<name> <- javg(...) #> For the full distribution (min, max, SD), run jdesc() on the new column.  # Fixed denominator (always divide by total number of variables) community$EnvAvg <- javg(Environment1, Environment3, Environment4,                          min.valid = 2, fixed = TRUE) #> Mean of 3 variables computed for 100 cases (fixed denominator) (min.valid = 2: 12 cases used partial data, 6 set to NA due to missing values). #> Mean of the new variable: 2.968. #>  #> Note: javg() returns the scores; assign them to a column to keep them: #>   community$<name> <- javg(...) #> For the full distribution (min, max, SD), run jdesc() on the new column.  # With a custom variable label community$ScaleMean <- javg(Environment1:Environment5,                             var.label = "Environment Scale Mean") #> Mean of 5 variables computed for 100 cases (18 set to NA due to missing values). #> Mean of the new variable: 2.995. #>  #> Note: javg() returns the scores; assign them to a column to keep them: #>   community$<name> <- javg(...) #> For the full distribution (min, max, SD), run jdesc() on the new column.  # With an explicit data frame (instead of using juse default) community$EnvAvg <- javg(community, Environment1, Environment3,                          Environment4) #> Mean of 3 variables computed for 100 cases (18 set to NA due to missing values). #> Mean of the new variable: 3.073. #>  #> Note: javg() returns the scores; assign them to a column to keep them: #>   community$<name> <- javg(...) #> For the full distribution (min, max, SD), run jdesc() on the new column.  # Not normally needed. You'd clear a default or registration only to # undo a mistake, or -- as in this example -- to reset state for testing. juse(NULL) #> Default data frame cleared.

what: Set a listwise complete-case filter for matching N across analyses — jcomplete
title: Set a listwise complete-case filter for matching N across analyses — jcomplete
text: jcomplete() registers set variables activates listwise deletion filter excludes case missing value registered variables. ensures subsequent analyses use set complete cases, essential preliminary analyses need match N final regression model. setting stored per dataset, switching juse() datasets preserves dataset's setting independently. jcomplete filter applies whenever matching dataset used, regardless whether supplied via juse() specified explicitly function call. bypass temporarily without losing setting, use jcomplete() analysis jcomplete() afterward. matches SPSS USE / FILTER convention.

title: Set a listwise complete-case filter for matching N across analyses — jcomplete
code: jcomplete(data, ..., preview = FALSE, console = FALSE, non.deletes = FALSE)

title: Set a listwise complete-case filter for matching N across analyses — jcomplete
text: data data frame. omitted, uses default set juse(). Pass NULL clear filter entirely. Pass bare word deactivate, reactivate. Call arguments check current status. ... Unquoted variable names include listwise check. preview Logical. TRUE, open viewer (RStudio data tab) showing rows listwise filter drop, leading Row column (original data position) trailing DeletionCheck flag (1 = row dropped). May used preview already-set filter without re-listing variables (jcomplete(preview = TRUE)). Default FALSE. console Logical numeric. Print dropped rows console. TRUE prints first 10; number prints many. Independent preview: prints console without opening viewer; combine preview = TRUE get . console listing always limited dropped rows flood console. Default FALSE. non.deletes Logical. TRUE, viewer shows every case (DeletionCheck marking drop) rather dropped rows. Affects viewer ; console listing stays deleted-rows-. Default FALSE.

title: Set a listwise complete-case filter for matching N across analyses — jcomplete
text: Invisibly returns NULL. preview requested, invisibly returns previewed data frame instead, can captured (e.g. jcomplete_rows <- jcomplete(preview = TRUE)).

title: Set a listwise complete-case filter for matching N across analyses — jcomplete
code: juse(community) #> Default data frame set to: community jcomplete(Income, Education, Age) #> Listwise Case Filter #> Using default data frame: community #>  #> Variable     N  Missing  % Missing #> ---------  ---  -------  --------- #> Income     100        6  6.0%      #> Education  100        6  6.0%      #> Age        100        0  0.0%      #>  #>   Complete cases: 88 of 100 (88.0%) #>   Listwise filter activated — 12 cases will be excluded from subsequent analyses. jdesc(Age)                     # Uses only complete cases on those 3 vars #> Descriptive Statistics #> Using default data frame: community #>  #> Case Processing  Excluded  Remaining #>     Original            —        100 #>     jcomplete          12         88  Income, Education, +1 more #>     Remaining N         —         88 #>  #> ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #>  #>  #> Variable  Total  Non_missing  Min  Max    Mean      SD #> --------  -----  -----------  ---  ---  ------  ------ #> Age          88           88   18   71  41.125  11.794 #>  jcomplete(Income, Education, Age, preview = TRUE)  # Set and preview together #> Listwise Case Filter #> Using default data frame: community #>  #> Variable     N  Missing  % Missing #> ---------  ---  -------  --------- #> Income     100        6  6.0%      #> Education  100        6  6.0%      #> Age        100        0  0.0%      #>  #>   Complete cases: 88 of 100 (88.0%) #>   Listwise filter activated — 12 cases will be excluded from subsequent analyses. #> jcomplete Preview — rows scheduled for deletion #> Row  Income  Education  Age  DeletionCheck #> ---  ------  ---------  ---  ------------- #>   2                  4   32              1 #>  13                  3   33              1 #>  19   14000              24              1 #>  27                  5   49              1 #>  30                  2   58              1 #>  35   56000              33              1 #>  41                  2   26              1 #>  45   46000              29              1 #>  48   55000              49              1 #>  74   57000              36              1 #>  #>   Showing the first 10 of 12 dropped rows. #> (The preview viewer needs an interactive RStudio session; showing the first 10 in the console instead.) jcomplete(preview = TRUE)      # Preview the already-set filter (viewer) #> jcomplete Preview — rows scheduled for deletion #> Row  Income  Education  Age  DeletionCheck #> ---  ------  ---------  ---  ------------- #>   2                  4   32              1 #>  13                  3   33              1 #>  19   14000              24              1 #>  27                  5   49              1 #>  30                  2   58              1 #>  35   56000              33              1 #>  41                  2   26              1 #>  45   46000              29              1 #>  48   55000              49              1 #>  74   57000              36              1 #>  #>   Showing the first 10 of 12 dropped rows. #> (The preview viewer needs an interactive RStudio session; showing the first 10 in the console instead.) jcomplete(preview = TRUE, non.deletes = TRUE)  # Viewer shows all cases #> jcomplete Preview — rows scheduled for deletion #> Row  Income  Education  Age  DeletionCheck #> ---  ------  ---------  ---  ------------- #>   2                  4   32              1 #>  13                  3   33              1 #>  19   14000              24              1 #>  27                  5   49              1 #>  30                  2   58              1 #>  35   56000              33              1 #>  41                  2   26              1 #>  45   46000              29              1 #>  48   55000              49              1 #>  74   57000              36              1 #>  #>   Showing the first 10 of 12 dropped rows. #> (The preview viewer needs an interactive RStudio session; showing the first 10 in the console instead.) jcomplete(console = 10)        # Console only -- first 10 dropped rows #> jcomplete Preview — rows scheduled for deletion #> Row  Income  Education  Age  DeletionCheck #> ---  ------  ---------  ---  ------------- #>   2                  4   32              1 #>  13                  3   33              1 #>  19   14000              24              1 #>  27                  5   49              1 #>  30                  2   58              1 #>  35   56000              33              1 #>  41                  2   26              1 #>  45   46000              29              1 #>  48   55000              49              1 #>  74   57000              36              1 #>  #>   Showing the first 10 of 12 dropped rows. Use preview = TRUE to see them all in the viewer. jcomplete(preview = TRUE, console = 25)        # Viewer and console #> jcomplete Preview — rows scheduled for deletion #> Row  Income  Education  Age  DeletionCheck #> ---  ------  ---------  ---  ------------- #>   2                  4   32              1 #>  13                  3   33              1 #>  19   14000              24              1 #>  27                  5   49              1 #>  30                  2   58              1 #>  35   56000              33              1 #>  41                  2   26              1 #>  45   46000              29              1 #>  48   55000              49              1 #>  74   57000              36              1 #>  84                  2   32              1 #>  95   58000              46              1 jcomplete(off)                 # Deactivate #> jcomplete deactivated for community. jcomplete(on)                  # Reactivate #> jcomplete reactivated for community: Income, Education, Age jcomplete()                    # Check status #> jcomplete active for community: Income, Education, Age (88 of 100 complete cases) jcomplete(NULL)                # Clear entirely #> jcomplete cleared for community (had: Income, Education, Age). # Not normally needed. You'd clear a default or registration only to # undo a mistake, or -- as in this example -- to reset state for testing. juse(NULL) #> Default data frame cleared.

what: Convert user-defined missing value (UDM) declarations between formats — jconvert
title: Convert user-defined missing value (UDM) declarations between formats — jconvert
text: jconvert() provides single entry point changing user- defined missing values (UDMs) represented columns data frame already memory. Three target formats supported: SPSS-style (na_values haven_labelled_spss), Stata-style (tagged_na haven_labelled), base R (declarations stripped, declared cells converted plain NA). Replaces jstrip_udm() (retired v0.9.5); base R target strip behavior.

title: Convert user-defined missing value (UDM) declarations between formats — jconvert
code: jconvert(data, to = NULL, ..., vars = NULL, udm.notice = TRUE)

title: Convert user-defined missing value (UDM) declarations between formats — jconvert
text: data data frame, omitted use juse() default. One "baseR", "spss", "stata" (case-sensitive). NULL (default), jconvert() reads joptions("missing.convention"): slot set "spss" "stata", resolves value; slot "none" default, jconvert() errors guidance naming three concrete options. destructive "baseR" target never auto-resolved – must always passed explicitly. ... Optional unquoted variable names. supplied, listed variables scanned. Mutually exclusive vars. vars Alternative scope--vector path: character vector variable names. Mutually exclusive .... ... vars empty, jconvert() operates whole data frame. udm.notice Logical; TRUE (default) prints notification summarizing converted (skipped) along assignment-syntax reminder. FALSE suppresses message. Always-default; consult joutput() function reports action just performed rather explaining system behavior.

title: Convert user-defined missing value (UDM) declarations between formats — jconvert
text: data frame requested conversions applied, returned invisibly. jrelabel() jrecode(), user must assign return value back retain changes.

title: Convert user-defined missing value (UDM) declarations between formats — jconvert
text: three target formats: = "baseR" Strip UDM declarations convert declared cells plain NA. SPSS-form columns (na_values / na_range haven_labelled_spss), masks declared codes NA removes attributes; value labels preserved column can still round-trip jsave() original labeling. columns carrying Stata-style missing values (tagged_na markers), uses haven::zap_missing() convert plain NAs. = "spss" Convert Stata-style SAS-style missing values SPSS-form numeric codes. Letter tags map numeric codes via joptions("udm.convention.codes") (default -99, -98, -97): .-> codes[1], .b -> codes[2], . SAS-style (uppercase) tags case-corrected Stata-style (lowercase) numeric mapping – round-trip purposes package treats ..conceptual marker, mixed-case columns collapse single lowercase marker (SPSS parallel uppercase convention). notification's per-column display shows original (pre-correction) tag SAS-corrected columns – e.g. ."Refused" -> -99 – user-visible mapping reflects actually data input. Letter tags beyond .d (case correction) refused guidance use jrecode() manual mapping. = "stata" Convert SPSS-form numeric codes Stata-style missing values. Letter tags assigned ordering rather convention: column's declared na_values codes sorted absolute value descending (ties broken -negative-first), mapped ., .b, .c order. Convention codes consulted direction; govern reverse (Stata SPSS) mapping. Round-trip conversions guaranteed preserve original numeric codes (e.g. SPSS c(-1, 9) -> Stata ., .b -> SPSS c(-99, -98) loses original numbers), value labels survive intact missingness semantics preserved. Range-based SPSS missings (na_range) cross-format scope; columns na_range refused guidance enumerate range SPSS first. Columns 4 distinct na_values codes also refused (matches 4-code cap Stata letter-tag mapping). Pre-flight checks = "spss" include collision check: column's target numeric code (e.g. -99 .) present genuine data column, call errors data touched. error message lists every colliding column presents three resolution paths: change convention codes via joptions(udm.convention.codes = ...), scope call via vars = c(...) exclude affected columns, recode real- data values via jrecode() first. Atomicity applies every error mode – entire jconvert() call either succeeds errors mutating data frame. Pattern – value labels suggest missingness formal declaration. column formal UDM declaration carries value labels matching package's missing-label wordlist (e.g. "Refused", "know", "applicable"), jconvert() skips column surfaces notification affected value/label pairs. formalise UDMs use jdeclare_udm(); leave ordinary data, action needed.

title: Convert user-defined missing value (UDM) declarations between formats — jconvert
code: # community ships with SPSS-form UDMs (Income, Education, Smoker, # Environment1, Environment3), so the conversions run on it directly.  # Strip UDMs from every applicable variable: df <- jconvert(community, to = "baseR") #> Stripped declarations of user-defined missing values (UDMs) from 5 variables: #>   Income        (-99 "Refused", -98 "Don't know") #>   Education     (-99 "Refused", -98 "Don't know") #>   Smoker        (-99 "Refused") #>   Environment1  (-99 "Refused", -98 "Don't know") #>   Environment3  (-99 "Refused", -98 "Don't know") #>  #> Assign the result to keep the conversion: #>   community <- jconvert(community, ...) #>  #> To keep it across sessions, save the data frame: #>   jsave(community, "community.rds")  # Convert SPSS-form UDMs to Stata-style missing values: df <- jconvert(community, to = "stata") #> Converted to Stata-style missing values in 5 variables: #>   Income        (-99 "Refused" -> .a, -98 "Don't know" -> .b) #>   Education     (-99 "Refused" -> .a, -98 "Don't know" -> .b) #>   Smoker        (-99 "Refused" -> .a) #>   Environment1  (-99 "Refused" -> .a, -98 "Don't know" -> .b) #>   Environment3  (-99 "Refused" -> .a, -98 "Don't know" -> .b) #>  #> Assign the result to keep the conversion: #>   community <- jconvert(community, ...) #>  #> To keep it across sessions, save the data frame: #>   jsave(community, "community.rds")  # Scope by unquoted names: df <- jconvert(community, to = "baseR", Income, Education) #> Stripped declarations of user-defined missing values (UDMs) from 2 variables: #>   Income     (-99 "Refused", -98 "Don't know") #>   Education  (-99 "Refused", -98 "Don't know") #>  #> Assign the result to keep the conversion: #>   community <- jconvert(community, ...) #>  #> To keep it across sessions, save the data frame: #>   jsave(community, "community.rds")  # Scope by character vector (alternative form): df <- jconvert(community, to = "baseR", vars = c("Income", "Education")) #> Stripped declarations of user-defined missing values (UDMs) from 2 variables: #>   Income     (-99 "Refused", -98 "Don't know") #>   Education  (-99 "Refused", -98 "Don't know") #>  #> Assign the result to keep the conversion: #>   community <- jconvert(community, ...) #>  #> To keep it across sessions, save the data frame: #>   jsave(community, "community.rds")  # Suppress the notification (e.g. inside a script): df <- jconvert(community, to = "baseR", udm.notice = FALSE)  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Convert with target inferred from joptions: joptions(missing.convention = "spss") df <- jconvert(df)   # converts any Stata-form columns to SPSS } # }

title: Bivariate correlation matrix with p values and pairwise N — jcorr

title: Bivariate correlation matrix with p values and pairwise N — jcorr
text: data data frame. ... Unquoted variable names within data. method Character. Correlation method: "pearson" (default), "spearman", "kendall". subset optional unquoted logical expression (e.g. Group == 1) subset cases call . Applied jcomplete jsubset. affect function calls. variable.id Character NULL. Variable label display mode: one "", "names", "labels", "legend", "legend.bottom". "names" shows variable names ; "" shows "name: label"; "labels" shows variable labels matrix row/column headers (honored even matrix grows wide – best short labels; rerun legend mode otherwise); "legend"/"legend.bottom" keep names print label legend table. Correlation requires numeric variables; supplying categorical raises error pointing jcrosstab() association categorical variables. (jcorr() handle asserted-categorical variable parked design decision.) count Optional character vector variable names treat counts call (per-call counterpart jcount()). count numeric-like , behaves like numeric: suppresses "seems categorical" caution variables. value.id supported jcorr(). function display value labels, passing argument error. exists return clear message rather misreporting token missing variable. Leave NULL (default). layout Character NULL. correlation cell laid three variables given: "wide" (default) puts r p-value one line N second line beneath; "stacked" places r, p, N three separate lines, giving narrower table fits variables wrapping. Ignored single pair (two variables), always prints one-line summary. NULL (default) defers corr.layout setting joptions() (defaulting "wide"). case.processing.detail Per-call override Case Processing Summary detail tier: one "none", "totals", "per_code". NULL (default) uses active joutput() level default. digits Integer NULL. Number decimal places continuous statistics output tables (range 0-7; digits = 0 prints whole numbers trailing decimal point). affect p-values, percentages, integer quantities (counts, N, degrees freedom), keep fixed conventions. NULL (default) defers joutput()'s digits setting (default 3).

title: Register variables as counts for analysis — jcount

One unquoted variable names register. remove Logical; TRUE, remove count registration named variables instead adding . clear.Logical; TRUE, clear count registrations every data frame carries .

title: Register variables as counts for analysis — jcount
text: variable carries exactly one registered intent time, registering count clears prior dummy numeric registration. Registration changes data assigns nothing. stored session, keyed data frame's name; save data frame R format (.rds) keep across sessions.

title: Register variables as counts for analysis — jcount
code: df <- data.frame(arrests = c(0, 1, 2, 0, 3, 1, 0, 12),                  age      = c(21, 34, 45, 29, 51, 38, 26, 60)) jcount(df, arrests)                 # treat as a count (here 0-12) #> Count registration set for 'arrests' in df. #> Note: this registration is stored for this session only. #> To keep it across sessions, save the data frame in R format (.rds): #>   jsave(df, "df.rds") #>  #> Next session, load that file to restore the registration: #>   df <- jload("df.rds") jcount(df, arrests, remove = TRUE) #> Count registration removed for 'arrests' in df. jcount()                            # list all registrations #> No variable registrations in this session. jcount(df, NULL)                    # clear df's count registrations #> No count registrations to clear for df. jcount(clear.all = TRUE)            # clear every frame's count registrations #> No count registrations to clear.

what: Cross-tabulation with optional chi-square test of independence — jcrosstab
title: Cross-tabulation with optional chi-square test of independence — jcrosstab
text: Produces cross-tabulation two categorical variables, showing observed frequencies row percentages default. Column percentages, expected frequencies, adjusted standardized residuals, chi-square test independence available via arguments. Handles haven-labelled, numeric, factor, character variables. haven-labelled variables, numeric codes displayed alongside labels.

title: Cross-tabulation with optional chi-square test of independence — jcrosstab
code: jcrosstab(   formula,   data,   chisq = FALSE,   expected = FALSE,   row.pct = TRUE,   col.pct = FALSE,   residuals = "none",   subset = NULL,   variable.id = NULL,   value.id = NULL,   case.processing.detail = NULL,   digits = NULL )

title: Cross-tabulation with optional chi-square test of independence — jcrosstab
text: formula formula form Row ~ Column. data data frame containing variables referenced formula. chisq Logical. TRUE, prints chi-square test independence cross-tabulation. Default FALSE. expected Logical. TRUE, prints expected frequencies alongside observed. Default FALSE. row.pct Logical. TRUE (default), shows row percentages. col.pct Logical. TRUE, shows column percentages. Default FALSE. residuals Character. Cell residuals display: "none" (default) "adjusted". "adjusted" adds (Adj.Res.) line cell showing adjusted standardized (Haberman) residual: (observed - expected) divided standard error. independence approximately standard normal, value beyond +/-1.96 flags cell whose count departs expected .05 level. localizes significant chi-square individual cells, matches "Adjusted standardized" residual SPSS CROSSTABS. joutput("full") residual cells flagged (* past +/-1.96, ** past Bonferroni cutoff) interpretation note printed table naming thresholds. logical. subset optional unquoted logical expression (e.g. Group == 1) subset cases call . Applied jcomplete jsubset. affect function calls. variable.id Character NULL. Variable label display mode: one "", "names", "labels", "legend", "legend.bottom". "names" shows variable names ; "" shows "name: label"; "labels" shows row/column variable labels (table header caption; cell value levels follow value.id mode) – best short labels; "legend"/"legend.bottom" keep names print label legend table. NULL (default) defers joutput()'s variable.id setting. logical. value.id Character NULL. Value-label display mode table axes: "" ("code: label"), "values" (bare code), "labels" (label, degrading bare code code none). "legend" "legend.bottom" keep bare code table print value-label legend ("legend" per-table, "legend.bottom" consolidated multiple tables produced). -op axis variables value labels. NULL (default) defers joutput()'s value.id setting. logical. case.processing.detail Per-call override Case Processing Summary detail tier: one "none", "totals", "per_code". NULL (default) uses active joutput() level default. digits Integer NULL. Number decimal places continuous statistics output tables (range 0-7; digits = 0 prints whole numbers trailing decimal point). affect p-values, percentages, integer quantities (counts, N, degrees freedom), keep fixed conventions. NULL (default) defers joutput()'s digits setting (default 3).

title: Cross-tabulation with optional chi-square test of independence — jcrosstab

what: Declare user-defined missing values on a variable — jdeclare_udm
title: Declare user-defined missing values on a variable — jdeclare_udm
text: jdeclare_udm() declares one user-defined missing values (UDMs) variable. UDMs specific data values – typically negative codes -99 Stata-style tagged markers .– indicate value missing (refused, know, applicable, etc.) rather simply missing. declared, UDM cells automatically excluded analyses remain visible data diagnostic purposes (see jfreq()). function operates declarative mode: call states column's complete UDM set. second call jdeclare_udm() column replaces, augments, prior declaration. matches SPSS's MISSING VALUES Stata's mvdecode semantics. prior UDMs dropped, note lists destructive aspect replacement silent.

title: Declare user-defined missing values on a variable — jdeclare_udm
code: jdeclare_udm(   data,   var,   codes = NULL,   labels = NULL,   convention = NULL,   udm.notice = TRUE )

title: Declare user-defined missing values on a variable — jdeclare_udm
text: data data frame containing variable. var variable declare UDMs (unquoted, e.g. Income). codes Numeric vector code values declare UDMs. Accepts two forms: Option (separate codes labels) Unnamed numeric vector; labels supplied via labels argument. E.g. codes = c(-99, -98), labels = "-99=Refused; -98=know". Option C (haven-style named vector) Named numeric vector; names labels. E.g. codes = c(Refused = -99, `know` = -98). Stata convention, code values may Stata-style missing-value markers created haven::tagged_na(), e.g. codes = c(Refused = tagged_na("")). labels Optional. quoted string form "value=label; value=label" pairing labels codes (Option ). Must NULL codes named (Option C). convention Optional. One "spss" "stata"; overrides convention resolution call. NULL (default), convention resolved column's existing UDM declaration (), joptions("missing.convention"), SPSS-form default. udm.notice Logical. TRUE (default), function prints notification summarizing declared. Set FALSE suppress.

title: Declare user-defined missing values on a variable — jdeclare_udm
text: data frame, specified variable updated carry declared UDMs.

title: Declare user-defined missing values on a variable — jdeclare_udm
text: SPSS convention, codes declared numeric values via column's na_values attribute (haven's representation SPSS-form UDMs). data cells unchanged; metadata flags certain values missing added. Stata convention Stata-style missing-value input, function attaches value labels existing Stata-style missing-value cells column. Stata convention numeric input, function converts matching cells Stata-style missing-value markers (Session 30 design lock). mapping ordering-based: codes sorted absolute value descending, -negative-first tie-breaker, assigned ., .b, .c, .d order. assignment proceeds independently joptions("udm.convention.codes") (governs reverse Stata--SPSS direction). conversion note standard/full joutput tier shows Stata-style equivalent future calls.

title: Declare user-defined missing values on a variable — jdeclare_udm
text: single data frame may carry SPSS-form Stata-form UDM columns. -memory analysis display tolerate mix without issue (column renders native form). constraint shows file-export time: .sav represent Stata-style missing values; .dta represent SPSS-form na_values declarations; .xpt can represent neither form. jsave() pre-flights DF destination format errors pointer jconvert() mix incompatible. post-declaration mismatch notice emitted bottom function's output exists alert early single-column declaration ends step rest DF.

title: Declare user-defined missing values on a variable — jdeclare_udm
code: # clinic$MoodRating arrives "dirty": -99/-98 sit in the data as # ordinary numbers (the state after a CSV or Excel import), so summary # statistics are poisoned until the codes are declared missing. df <- clinic jdesc(df, MoodRating)        # mean dragged far down by -99/-98 #> Descriptive Statistics #>  #> Variable    Total  Non_missing  Min  Max    Mean      SD #> ----------  -----  -----------  ---  ---  ------  ------ #> MoodRating     70           70  -99    9  -4.943  31.477 #>   # SPSS form: declare -99 and -98 as UDMs with labels df <- jdeclare_udm(df, MoodRating,                    codes  = c(-99, -98),                    labels = "-99=Refused; -98=Don't know") #> Declared SPSS-style missing values in: #>   df$MoodRating #>   -99 ["Refused"] #>   -98 ["Don't know"] #>  #> Assign the result to keep the declaration: #>   df <- jdeclare_udm(df, MoodRating, ...) #>  #> To keep it across sessions, save the data frame: #>   jsave(df, "df.rds") jdesc(df, MoodRating)        # codes now excluded as missing #> Descriptive Statistics #>  #> Case Processing  Excluded  Remaining #>     Original            —         70 #>     Remaining N         —         70 #>  #> ──────────────────────────────────── #>  #>  #> Variable    Total  Non_missing  Min  Max  Mean     SD #> ----------  -----  -----------  ---  ---  ----  ----- #> MoodRating     70           63    1    9  5.46  1.702 #>   # Equivalent using named codes (one step instead of codes + labels) df2 <- jdeclare_udm(clinic, MoodRating,                     codes = c("Refused" = -99, "Don't know" = -98)) #> Declared SPSS-style missing values in: #>   clinic$MoodRating #>   -99 ["Refused"] #>   -98 ["Don't know"] #>  #> Assign the result to keep the declaration: #>   clinic <- jdeclare_udm(clinic, MoodRating, ...) #>  #> To keep it across sessions, save the data frame: #>   jsave(clinic, "clinic.rds")  # Stata-style: label Stata-style missing-value cells. The jrecode() call # turns the literal codes into tagged cells; jdeclare_udm() labels them. df3 <- clinic df3$Mood2 <- jrecode(df3, MoodRating,                      map = "-99=.a; -98=.b; else=copy",                      convention = "stata") #>  #> Note: jrecode() returns the recoded values; assign them to a column to keep them: #>   df3$<name> <- jrecode(...) #> To check the recode landed correctly, compare jfreq() on the original and the new column. df3 <- jdeclare_udm(df3, Mood2,                     codes = c("Refused"    = haven::tagged_na("a"),                               "Don't know" = haven::tagged_na("b"))) #> Labelled Stata-style missing values in: #>   df3$Mood2 #>   .a ["Refused"] #>   .b ["Don't know"] #>  #> Assign the result to keep the declaration: #>   df3 <- jdeclare_udm(df3, Mood2, ...) #>  #> To keep it across sessions, save the data frame: #>   jsave(df3, "df3.rds") #> Note: variable Mood2 is Stata-style, but other columns in df3 are predominantly SPSS-style. #> Use jconvert() to align if desired.

what: Descriptive statistics for one or more variables — jdesc
title: Descriptive statistics for one or more variables — jdesc
text: Computes basic descriptive statistics (N, non-missing, min, max, mean, SD) one variables data frame. Prints formatted table invisibly returns underlying results data frame.

title: Descriptive statistics for one or more variables — jdesc
code: jdesc(   data,   ...,   by = NULL,   subset = NULL,   variable.id = NULL,   numeric = NULL,   categorical = NULL,   count = NULL,   value.id = NULL,   case.processing.detail = NULL,   digits = NULL )

title: Descriptive statistics for one or more variables — jdesc
text: data data frame, numeric vector. ... Unquoted variable names within data (ignored data vector). optional unquoted grouping variable name. provided, descriptives computed separately group, separate titled table per dependent variable. subset optional unquoted logical expression (e.g. Group == 1) subset cases call . Applied jcomplete jsubset. affect function calls. variable.id Character NULL. Variable label display mode: one "", "names", "labels", "legend", "legend.bottom". "names" shows variable names ; "" shows "name: label"; "labels" shows variable's label place name (descriptives table; grouped output, per-variable caption grouping-variable column header) – best short labels; "legend" "legend.bottom" keep names print label legend table. NULL (default) defers joutput()'s variable.id setting. logical. numeric Optional character vector variable names treat continuous call (per-call counterpart jnumeric()). effect jdesc() suppress structural "seems categorical" descriptive caution variables; descriptives computed way regardless. categorical supported jdesc() yet. jdesc() always computes numeric descriptives; supplying categorical raises error pointing jfreq() categorical summary. (jdesc() handle asserted-categorical variable parked design decision.) count Optional character vector variable names treat counts call (per-call counterpart jcount()). count numeric-like , behaves like numeric: suppresses "seems categorical" caution variables. value.id Character NULL. Value-label display mode grouped descriptive headers (-group rows): "" ("code: label"), "values" (bare code), "labels" (label, degrading bare code code none). "legend" "legend.bottom" keep bare code table print value-label legend ("legend" per-table, "legend.bottom" consolidated multiple tables produced). -op grouping variables value labels, ungrouped calls. NULL (default) defers joutput()'s value.id setting. logical. case.processing.detail Per-call override Case Processing Summary detail tier: one "none", "totals", "per_code". NULL (default) uses active joutput() level default. digits Integer NULL. Number decimal places continuous statistics output tables (range 0-7; digits = 0 prints whole numbers trailing decimal point). affect p-values, percentages, integer quantities (counts, N, degrees freedom), keep fixed conventions. NULL (default) defers joutput()'s digits setting (default 3).

title: Descriptive statistics for one or more variables — jdesc
text: Invisibly returns list class jst_desc containing: descriptives (data frame statistics, NULL grouped output), sample_info (pipeline missing data counts). Also prints formatted table console.

title: Descriptive statistics for one or more variables — jdesc
text: Output structured consistently jfreq(): red title printed first, followed block showing type variable label ("None" label present) variable, single blank line table. multiple variables, one type/label entry printed per variable shared table. Summarizes numeric, haven-labelled, logical, numeric-coded factor, numeric-looking character variables. Variables summarized — text factors, text character variables, date/time variables — skipped warning directing user jfreq() (date/time variables supported ). every requested variable unsummarizable, jdesc() stops error. Also accepts simple numeric vector. Supports grouped descriptives via parameter. Haven-labelled variables reported haven_labelled (Categorical) type line; uninformative vctrs_vctr class suppressed.

title: Descriptive statistics for one or more variables — jdesc
code: # With explicit data frame jdesc(community, Age) #> Descriptive Statistics #>  #> Variable  Total  Non_missing  Min  Max   Mean     SD #> --------  -----  -----------  ---  ---  -----  ----- #> Age         100          100   18   71  40.66  11.68 #>  jdesc(community, Income, Age, WellbeingScore) #> Descriptive Statistics #>  #> Case Processing  Excluded  Remaining #>     Original            —        100 #>     Remaining N         —        100 #>  #> ──────────────────────────────────── #>  #>  #> Variable        Total  Non_missing    Min    Max       Mean         SD #> --------------  -----  -----------  -----  -----  ---------  --------- #> Income            100           94  14000  91000  47414.894  20145.391 #> Age               100          100     18     71     40.660     11.680 #> WellbeingScore    100          100     27     77     50.600     11.411 #>  jdesc(community, WellbeingScore, by = Volunteer) #> Descriptive Statistics by Volunteer (2 levels) #>  #> WellbeingScore #>  #> Volunteer   N  Min  Max    Mean      SD #> ---------  --  ---  ---  ------  ------ #> 0: No      58   27   70  46.431  10.318 #> 1: Yes     42   32   77  56.357  10.385 #>  #>   # Using juse() default juse(community) #> Default data frame set to: community jdesc(Age) #> Descriptive Statistics #> Using default data frame: community #>  #> Variable  Total  Non_missing  Min  Max   Mean     SD #> --------  -----  -----------  ---  ---  -----  ----- #> Age         100          100   18   71  40.66  11.68 #>  jdesc(Income, Age, WellbeingScore) #> Descriptive Statistics #> Using default data frame: community #>  #> Case Processing  Excluded  Remaining #>     Original            —        100 #>     Remaining N         —        100 #>  #> ──────────────────────────────────── #>  #>  #> Variable        Total  Non_missing    Min    Max       Mean         SD #> --------------  -----  -----------  -----  -----  ---------  --------- #> Income            100           94  14000  91000  47414.894  20145.391 #> Age               100          100     18     71     40.660     11.680 #> WellbeingScore    100          100     27     77     50.600     11.411 #>  jdesc(WellbeingScore, by = Volunteer) #> Descriptive Statistics by Volunteer (2 levels) #> Using default data frame: community #>  #> WellbeingScore #>  #> Volunteer   N  Min  Max    Mean      SD #> ---------  --  ---  ---  ------  ------ #> 0: No      58   27   70  46.431  10.318 #> 1: Yes     42   32   77  56.357  10.385 #>  #>   # With a vector directly jdesc(community$Age) #> Descriptive Statistics #>  #> Variable  Total  Non_missing  Min  Max   Mean     SD #> --------  -----  -----------  ---  ---  -----  ----- #> Age         100          100   18   71  40.66  11.68 #>

what: Register categorical variables for dummy coding in regression — jdummy
title: Register categorical variables for dummy coding in regression — jdummy
text: jdummy() registers categorical variable jlm() automatically expands dummy (indicator) variables appears regression formula. original data frame never modified. Several variables can registered one call; ref setting applies . Registrations stored per dataset, switching juse() datasets preserves dataset's registrations independently.

title: Register categorical variables for dummy coding in regression — jdummy
code: jdummy(   data,   ...,   ref = "first",   show = FALSE,   remove = FALSE,   clear.all = FALSE,   max.categories = 20L )

title: Register categorical variables for dummy coding in regression — jdummy
text: data data frame, omit use juse() default. jdummy(NULL) clears dummy registrations juse() default data frame (, default set, frame carries ; several , asks rather wiping ). ... One unquoted variable names register. Omit (along data) display current registrations. lone NULL variable slot – jdummy(data, NULL) – clears frame's dummy registrations. ref reference category (excluded regression model). Can numeric code, quoted label name, first (default) last. Applied every variable named call; use different reference categories, register variables separate calls. show Logical. TRUE, prints dummy coding scheme table showing pattern 0s 1s. Default FALSE. remove Logical. TRUE, removes registration specified variable(s). Default FALSE. clear.Logical. TRUE, clears dummy registrations every data frame carries . Default FALSE. max.categories Integer. Maximum number categories variable may dummy-coded; variable raises error. Raise dummy-code higher-cardinality variable. Default 20L.

title: Register categorical variables for dummy coding in regression — jdummy
text: Invisibly returns NULL. Called side effect.

title: Register categorical variables for dummy coding in regression — jdummy
code: juse(community) #> Default data frame set to: community jdummy(Region)                       # Register, first category as reference #> Dummy Variable Registration #> Using default data frame: community #>  #>   Variable: Region (haven_labelled) #>   Reference category: Region_North #>   Dummy variables: Region_South, Region_East, Region_West #>   Cases: 100 (0 missing) #>  #> Note: this registration is stored for this session only. #> To keep it across sessions, save the data frame in R format (.rds): #>   jsave(community, "community.rds") #>  #> Next session, load that file to restore the registration: #>   community <- jload("community.rds") jdummy(Region, Education)            # Register several at once #> Dummy Variable Registration #> Using default data frame: community #>  #>   Variable: Region (haven_labelled) #>   Reference category: Region_North #>   Dummy variables: Region_South, Region_East, Region_West #>   Cases: 100 (0 missing) #>  #>   Variable: Education (haven_labelled) #>   Reference category: Education_Some_high_school #>   Dummy variables: Education_High_school_graduate, Education_Some_college, Education_Bachelor_s_degree, Education_Graduate_degree #>   Cases: 100 (6 missing) #>  #> Note: registrations are stored for this session only. #> To keep them across sessions, save the data frame in R format (.rds): #>   jsave(community, "community.rds") #>  #> Next session, load that file to restore the registrations: #>   community <- jload("community.rds") jdummy(Region, ref = "last")         # Last category as reference #> Dummy Variable Registration #> Using default data frame: community #>  #>   Variable: Region (haven_labelled) #>   Reference category: Region_West #>   Dummy variables: Region_North, Region_South, Region_East #>   Cases: 100 (0 missing) #>  #> Note: this registration is stored for this session only. #> To keep it across sessions, save the data frame in R format (.rds): #>   jsave(community, "community.rds") #>  #> Next session, load that file to restore the registration: #>   community <- jload("community.rds") jdummy(Region, ref = 4)              # Reference by numeric code #> Dummy Variable Registration #> Using default data frame: community #>  #>   Variable: Region (haven_labelled) #>   Reference category: Region_West #>   Dummy variables: Region_North, Region_South, Region_East #>   Cases: 100 (0 missing) #>  #> Note: this registration is stored for this session only. #> To keep it across sessions, save the data frame in R format (.rds): #>   jsave(community, "community.rds") #>  #> Next session, load that file to restore the registration: #>   community <- jload("community.rds") jdummy(Region, ref = "East")         # Reference by value label #> Dummy Variable Registration #> Using default data frame: community #>  #>   Variable: Region (haven_labelled) #>   Reference category: Region_East #>   Dummy variables: Region_North, Region_South, Region_West #>   Cases: 100 (0 missing) #>  #> Note: this registration is stored for this session only. #> To keep it across sessions, save the data frame in R format (.rds): #>   jsave(community, "community.rds") #>  #> Next session, load that file to restore the registration: #>   community <- jload("community.rds") jdummy(Region, show = TRUE)          # Show coding scheme #> Dummy Variable Registration #> Using default data frame: community #>  #>   Variable: Region (haven_labelled) #>   Reference category: Region_East #>   Dummy variables: Region_North, Region_South, Region_West #>   Cases: 100 (0 missing) #>  #>   Dummy Coding Scheme: #>  #>                      Region_North  Region_South  Region_East*  Region_West #>     ---------------  ------------  ------------  ------------  ----------- #>     1: Region_North             1             0             0            0 #>     2: Region_South             0             1             0            0 #>     3: Region_East*             0             0             1            0 #>     4: Region_West              0             0             0            1 #>  #>     * Reference category #>  jdummy(Region, show = "all")         # Full scheme (for many categories) #> Dummy Variable Registration #> Using default data frame: community #>  #>   Variable: Region (haven_labelled) #>   Reference category: Region_East #>   Dummy variables: Region_North, Region_South, Region_West #>   Cases: 100 (0 missing) #>  #>   Dummy Coding Scheme: #>  #>                      Region_North  Region_South  Region_East*  Region_West #>     ---------------  ------------  ------------  ------------  ----------- #>     1: Region_North             1             0             0            0 #>     2: Region_South             0             1             0            0 #>     3: Region_East*             0             0             1            0 #>     4: Region_West              0             0             0            1 #>  #>     * Reference category #>  jdummy()                             # Show all registrations #> Dummy Variable Registrations #> Using default data frame: community #>  #>   Variable: Region (haven_labelled) #>   Reference category: 3: Region_East #>   Dummy variables: Region_North, Region_South, Region_West #>   Cases: 100 (0 missing) #>  #>   Variable: Education (haven_labelled) #>   Reference category: 1: Education_Some_high_school #>   Dummy variables: Education_High_school_graduate, Education_Some_college, Education_Bachelor_s_degree, Education_Graduate_degree #>   Cases: 100 (6 missing) #>  jdummy(Region, remove = TRUE)        # Remove one registration #> Dummy registration removed for 'Region' in community. jdummy(community, NULL)              # Clear community's dummy registrations #> Dummy registrations cleared for community: Education. jdummy(NULL)                         # Clear the default frame's (or ask) #> No dummy registrations to clear for community (the default data frame). jdummy(clear.all = TRUE)             # Clear every frame's dummy registrations #> No dummy registrations to clear. # Not normally needed. You'd clear a default or registration only to # undo a mistake, or -- as in this example -- to reset state for testing. juse(NULL) #> Default data frame cleared.

what: SPSS-like frequency tables for categorical variables — jfreq
title: SPSS-like frequency tables for categorical variables — jfreq
text: Prints SPSS-style frequency table (Freq, Total %, Valid %, Cum. %) variable supplied. Designed use unquoted variable names, also accepts plain vector.

title: SPSS-like frequency tables for categorical variables — jfreq
code: jfreq(   data,   ...,   subset = NULL,   variable.id = NULL,   value.id = NULL,   case.processing.detail = NULL )

title: SPSS-like frequency tables for categorical variables — jfreq
text: data data frame, vector. ... Unquoted variable name(s) within data (ignored data vector). subset optional unquoted logical expression (e.g. Group == 1) subset cases call . Applied jcomplete jsubset. affect function calls. variable.id Character NULL. Variable label display mode: one "", "names", "labels", "legend", "legend.bottom". "names" shows variable names ; "" shows "name: label"; "labels" uses variable's label table caption (best short labels); "legend" prints label legend variable's table; "legend.bottom" prints one consolidated legend tables. NULL (default) defers joutput()'s variable.id setting. logical. (Replaces former inline Type/label block.) value.id Character NULL. Value-label display mode frequency-table valid rows: "" ("code: label"), "values" (bare code), "labels" (label, degrading bare code code none). "legend" "legend.bottom" keep bare code table print value-label legend ("legend" per-table, "legend.bottom" consolidated multiple tables produced). -op variables value labels. NULL (default) defers joutput()'s value.id setting. logical. case.processing.detail Accepted API symmetry. jfreq's Case Processing Summary top-table (missing-data breakdown), argument effect; per-variable code detail already appears variable's frequency table.

title: SPSS-like frequency tables for categorical variables — jfreq
text: Invisibly returns list class jst_freq containing: frequencies (named list data frames, one per variable) sample_info (pipeline missing data counts).

title: SPSS-like frequency tables for categorical variables — jfreq
text: Output structured consistently jdesc(): single red "Frequencies" title printed first, followed default-data note (juse() default used), pipeline messages, Case Processing Summary (least one pipeline stage active call). variable gets block consisting variable name line, indented Type Variable label lines (suppressed joutput()'s variable.id toggle ), blank line, frequency table. frequency table ends Total row showing post-pipeline N. haven-labelled variables, value labels numeric codes combined frequency table rows (e.g. 1: Strongly Oppose). type line reports haven_labelled (Categorical) suppresses uninformative vctrs_vctr class. Variable labels shown variable types, haven-labelled ones.

what: Register variables as Likert (ordered response) items — jlikert
title: Register variables as Likert (ordered response) items — jlikert
text: jlikert() declares one value-labelled variables Likert items – ordered response scales (example 1 = Strongly disagree 5 = Strongly agree). ordered-scale counterpart jdummy (categorical), jnumeric (continuous), jcount (count): variable carries exactly one registered intent time, registering Likert clears prior numeric, count, dummy registration .

title: Register variables as Likert (ordered response) items — jlikert
Register variables as Likert (ordered response) items — jlikert
text: data data frame, omitted use juse default. ... One unquoted variable names register, single NULL clear frame's Likert registrations (see Details). remove Logical; TRUE, remove named variables' Likert registrations instead adding . clear.Logical; TRUE, clear Likert registrations every data frame.

title: Register variables as Likert (ordered response) items — jlikert
text: Invisibly NULL. Called side effect session registry.

title: Register variables as Likert (ordered response) items — jlikert
text: Scope – display . Likert intent refines reporting, analysis. sets variable's sub-class "Likert" jscreen's Variable Types table, marking ordered scale rather generic N-category variable. change analysis treats variable (order-aware modelling), change jplot output – value-labelled small-range variable already plots ordered, labelled bar regardless registration. Like registration verbs, registrations session-scoped keyed data-frame name; save frame R format (.rds) jsave keep across sessions. Clearing mirrors registration verbs: jlikert(data, NULL) – clear frame's Likert registrations. jlikert(NULL) – clear juse() default frame (sole frame carrying Likert registrations; several , asks rather clearing ). jlikert(clear.= TRUE) – clear every frame. jlikert() arguments prints current registration status.

title: Register variables as Likert (ordered response) items — jlikert
code: jlikert(community, Environment1, Environment2)  # declare two Likert items #> Likert registration set for 'Environment1', 'Environment2' in community. #> Note: registrations are stored for this session only. #> To keep them across sessions, save the data frame in R format (.rds): #>   jsave(community, "community.rds") #>  #> Next session, load that file to restore the registrations: #>   community <- jload("community.rds")   jscreen(community)                              # Sub-class shows "Likert" #> Data Screening #>   Cases: 100  #>   Variables: 15  #>   Cases with missing data: 30  #>   Variables with outliers: 0  #>  #> Variable Types #> Variable        jstats Class  Sub-class   Source         Unique Values #> --------------  ------------  ----------  -------------  ------------- #> RespondentID    Categorical   identifier                           100 #> Income          Numeric                                             52 #> Education       Categorical   5-category                             5 #> Age             Numeric                                             41 #> WellbeingScore  Numeric                                             43 #> Volunteer       Categorical   dichotomy                              2 #> OwnsHome        Categorical   dichotomy*                             2 #> Smoker          Categorical   dichotomy                              2 #> CommuteTime     Numeric                                             46 #> Region          Categorical   4-category                             4 #> Environment1    Categorical   Likert      User-declared              5 #> Environment2    Categorical   Likert      User-declared              5 #> Environment3    Categorical   Likert                                 5 #> Environment4    Categorical   Likert                                 5 #> Environment5    Categorical   Likert                                 5 #> * coded other than 0/1; mean is not a proportion #>  #> Missing Data & Outliers (outliers > 3 SD from mean) #> Variable      Missing  % Missing #> ------------  -------  --------- #> Income              6        6.0 #> Education           6        6.0 #> Smoker              5        5.0 #> Environment1       12       12.0 #> Environment3       12       12.0 #>    jlikert(community, Environment1, remove = TRUE) # undo one #> Likert registration removed for 'Environment1' in community.   jlikert(community, NULL)                        # clear the registrations #> Likert registrations cleared for community: Environment2.

what: SPSS-like linear regression output with standardized coefficients — jlm
title: SPSS-like linear regression output with standardized coefficients — jlm
text: Fits linear model using stats::lm() prints SPSS-style output, including unstandardized coefficients, standard errors, t values, p values, standardized coefficients (beta). Standardized coefficients left blank intercept dummy-coded categorical terms.

title: SPSS-like linear regression output with standardized coefficients — jlm
code: jlm(   formula,   data,   subset = NULL,   variable.id = NULL,   numeric = NULL,   categorical = NULL,   count = NULL,   ci = NULL,   std = "regular",   diagnostics = NULL,   ref.categories = NULL,   full = FALSE,   case.processing.detail = NULL,   digits = NULL,   ...,   value.id = NULL )

title: SPSS-like linear regression output with standardized coefficients — jlm
text: formula model formula, e.g. y ~ x1 + x2. data data frame containing variables referenced formula. subset optional unquoted logical expression (e.g. Group == 1) subset cases call . Applied jcomplete jsubset. affect function calls. variable.id Character NULL. Variable label display mode: one "", "names", "labels", "legend", "legend.bottom". "names" shows variable names ; "" shows "name: label"; "labels" replaces coefficient's variable name label Coefficients table (factor level decoration preserved) – best short labels; "legend" prints label legend Coefficients table R-squared/fit block; "legend.bottom" prints end. NULL (default) defers joutput()'s variable.id setting. logical. numeric Optional character vector variable names treated continuous (numeric) even value labels. example, numeric = "Age" numeric = c("Age", "Education"). categorical Optional character vector variable names treated categorical even lack value labels. example, categorical = "Program" categorical = c("Program", "Region"). first sorted unique value becomes reference category. Use jdummy() control reference category. count Optional character vector variable names treat counts call (per-call counterpart jcount()). dependent variable speaks count-regression caveat definitively rather hedge, applies even variable sits outside structural 0-6 band. independent variable behaves like numeric (count predictor enters model numeric). variable listed count categorical. ci Logical NULL. TRUE, appends 95% confidence interval unstandardized coefficient (b) right coefficient table. NULL (default), defers joutput()'s regression.ci setting (minimal standard, full). Computed closed form b +/- t(.975, residual df) * SE. std Character. Controls standardized-coefficient column.
