One "regular" (default) – standardized betas prevalence-scaled betas dummy dichotomous predictors suppressed, since fully standardized beta 0/1 indicator comparable continuous betas; "" – standardized betas nothing suppressed; "gelman" – Gelman (2008) scaling, continuous predictors placed divide--two-standard-deviations scale binary predictors keep raw 0/1 contrast (shown predictors, headed "Gelman beta"); "none" – omit column. returned object always carries full regular betas (beta) full Gelman betas (beta_gelman) regardless display choice. diagnostics Logical, character vector, NULL. TRUE, prints VIF table diagnostic plots. character vector, specifies diagnostics show: vif, residuals, qq, scale, cooks, leverage. NULL (default), defers joutput() session setting. ref.categories Logical NULL. Per-call override showing reference-categories block (baseline level dropped set dummy variables). NULL (default) defers joutput()'s ref.categories setting. Applies jlm() jlogistic() , since functions produce dummy-coded coefficient tables. full Logical. TRUE, turns coefficient confidence interval diagnostics. override explicit FALSE values. case.processing.detail Per-call override Case Processing Summary detail tier: one "none", "totals", "per_code". NULL (default) uses active joutput() level default. digits Integer NULL. Number decimal places continuous statistics output tables (range 0-7; digits = 0 prints whole numbers trailing decimal point). affect p-values, percentages, integer quantities (counts, N, degrees freedom), keep fixed conventions. NULL (default) defers joutput()'s digits setting (default 3). ... Reserved argument-name checking. Passing , plots, show produce helpful error suggesting diagnostics instead. value.id Character NULL. Value-label display mode dummy category rows Coefficients table: one "" ("code: label", degrading bare code code label), "values" (bare code), "labels" (value label, degrading bare code none exists). reference category folded grouped variable's header follows mode. "legend" "legend.bottom" supported : coefficient table already pairs value label row, separate legend block duplicate . Passing either explicitly error; joutput() default "legend" "legend.bottom" tolerated rendered "", break bare call. Variables value labels render identically supported modes. NULL (default) defers joutput()'s value.id setting. Applies multi-category dummy predictors; continuous single-contrast (dichotomous) predictors unaffected. logical.

title: SPSS-like linear regression output with standardized coefficients — jlm
text: Invisibly returns list class jst_lm containing: model fitted lm object. model_type Character string linear. model_frame model frame used fit model. formula_used formula dummy expansion. coefficients Formatted coefficient table (data frame); includes 95% CI Lower / Upper columns ci . coefficients_raw Flat data frame raw, full-precision coefficient statistics (one row per coefficient): term (machine key), b, SE, t, df, p, beta, ci_lower / ci_upper bounds (present regardless ci display toggle). Carries beta_standardization outcome attributes. fit_raw List raw, full-precision fit statistics (R-squared, adjusted R-squared, residual SE, F dfs p-value, residual df, N). r_squared R-squared value. adj_r_squared Adjusted R-squared value. residual_se Residual standard error. f_statistic Named numeric vector F value, df1, df2, p. sums_of_squares Named numeric vector (regression, residual, total). n Number observations used model. dummy_coef_names Names dummy variable columns created jdummy() registrations. ref_cats Reference category descriptions categorical variables model. vif Named numeric vector VIF values, NULL bivariate. sample_info Pipeline missing data counts.

title: SPSS-like linear regression output with standardized coefficients — jlm
text: Also prints key model summary information (R-squared, adjusted R-squared, residual standard error, F-test, sums squares, N). coefficients dropped due perfect collinearity, warning message printed. red "Linear Regression" title printed first, followed variable labels (present), coefficient table model fit statistics. Handling variables: Variables registered jdummy() expanded dummy variables using registered reference category. Unregistered haven-labelled variables value labels automatically treated categorical (converted factors). first category used reference, informational message suggests using jdummy() control reference category. Haven-labelled variables without value labels treated continuous (converted numeric). numeric argument overrides auto-detection variables value labels treated continuous (e.g. Age labels like "18 years", "19 years"). categorical argument forces variables without value labels (plain numeric variables) treated categorical (e.g. numeric Program variable coded 1–4 CSV file). dependent variable always modelled numeric. Naming numeric count change ; asserts DV's role count / categorical-like note silenced (numeric) stated definitively (count).

title: SPSS-like linear regression output with standardized coefficients — jlm
code: # With explicit data frame (named argument) jlm(WellbeingScore ~ Income + Age, data = community) #> Linear Regression #>  #> Case Processing    Excluded  Remaining #>     Original              —        100 #>     Auto-listwise         6         94 #>     Analysis N            —         94 #>  #> Missing-data breakdown  From 100    % #>     Income #>       Missing              6      6.0 #>  #> ────────────────────────────────────── #>  #>  #> Coefficients #>                b      SE      t      β      p   #> -----------  ------  -----  -----  -----  ----- #> (Intercept)  29.287  3.610  8.113         <.001 #> Income        0.000  0.000  6.416  0.549  <.001 #> Age           0.170  0.083  2.060  0.176   .042 #>  #> Outcome: WellbeingScore #>  #> R-squared: 0.387    Adjusted R-squared: 0.373 #> Residual Standard Error: 8.925 #>  #> F-statistic: 28.707 on 2 and 91 DF, p-value: <.001 #> Sum of Squares: #>   Regression: 4573.217 #>   Residual:   7248.527 #>   Total:      11821.745 #>   # With explicit data frame (positional argument) jlm(WellbeingScore ~ Income + Age, community) #> Linear Regression #>  #> Case Processing    Excluded  Remaining #>     Original              —        100 #>     Auto-listwise         6         94 #>     Analysis N            —         94 #>  #> Missing-data breakdown  From 100    % #>     Income #>       Missing              6      6.0 #>  #> ────────────────────────────────────── #>  #>  #> Coefficients #>                b      SE      t      β      p   #> -----------  ------  -----  -----  -----  ----- #> (Intercept)  29.287  3.610  8.113         <.001 #> Income        0.000  0.000  6.416  0.549  <.001 #> Age           0.170  0.083  2.060  0.176   .042 #>  #> Outcome: WellbeingScore #>  #> R-squared: 0.387    Adjusted R-squared: 0.373 #> Residual Standard Error: 8.925 #>  #> F-statistic: 28.707 on 2 and 91 DF, p-value: <.001 #> Sum of Squares: #>   Regression: 4573.217 #>   Residual:   7248.527 #>   Total:      11821.745 #>   # Using juse() default juse(community) #> Default data frame set to: community jlm(WellbeingScore ~ Income + Age) #> Linear Regression #> Using default data frame: community #>  #> Case Processing    Excluded  Remaining #>     Original              —        100 #>     Auto-listwise         6         94 #>     Analysis N            —         94 #>  #> Missing-data breakdown  From 100    % #>     Income #>       Missing              6      6.0 #>  #> ────────────────────────────────────── #>  #>  #> Coefficients #>                b      SE      t      β      p   #> -----------  ------  -----  -----  -----  ----- #> (Intercept)  29.287  3.610  8.113         <.001 #> Income        0.000  0.000  6.416  0.549  <.001 #> Age           0.170  0.083  2.060  0.176   .042 #>  #> Outcome: WellbeingScore #>  #> R-squared: 0.387    Adjusted R-squared: 0.373 #> Residual Standard Error: 8.925 #>  #> F-statistic: 28.707 on 2 and 91 DF, p-value: <.001 #> Sum of Squares: #>   Regression: 4573.217 #>   Residual:   7248.527 #>   Total:      11821.745 #>   # CATEGORICAL PREDICTORS # # Per-call: categorical = ... applies for one call only and does not # persist. Useful for a quick one-off analysis. jlm(WellbeingScore ~ Region + Age, categorical = "Region") #> Linear Regression #> Using default data frame: community #>  #> Coefficients #>                     b      SE      t       β      p   #> ----------------  ------  -----  ------  -----  ----- #> (Intercept)       37.016  4.326   8.557         <.001 #> Region_South (1)  -0.370  3.243  -0.114          .909 #> Region_East (1)   -0.388  2.903  -0.133          .894 #> Region_West (1)   -5.692  2.940  -1.936          .056 #> Age                0.376  0.095   3.962  0.385  <.001 #>  #> Outcome: WellbeingScore #>  #> R-squared: 0.169    Adjusted R-squared: 0.134 #> Residual Standard Error: 10.622 #>  #> F-statistic: 4.814 on 4 and 95 DF, p-value: .001 #> Sum of Squares: #>   Regression: 2172.440 #>   Residual:   10717.560 #>   Total:      12890.000 #>   # The recommended approach for repeated analyses: register the variable # with jdummy() before running jlm(). This sets the categorical # treatment persistently, so subsequent jlm() calls (and other # analyses) use the same coding without re-specifying. jdummy(community, Region) #> Dummy Variable Registration #>   Variable: Region (haven_labelled) #>   Reference category: Region_North #>   Dummy variables: Region_South, Region_East, Region_West #>   Cases: 100 (0 missing) #>  #> Note: this registration is stored for this session only. #> To keep it across sessions, save the data frame in R format (.rds): #>   jsave(community, "community.rds") #>  #> Next session, load that file to restore the registration: #>   community <- jload("community.rds") jlm(WellbeingScore ~ Region + Age) #> Linear Regression #> Using default data frame: community #>  #> Coefficients #>                     b      SE      t       β      p   #> ----------------  ------  -----  ------  -----  ----- #> (Intercept)       37.016  4.326   8.557         <.001 #> Region_South (1)  -0.370  3.243  -0.114          .909 #> Region_East (1)   -0.388  2.903  -0.133          .894 #> Region_West (1)   -5.692  2.940  -1.936          .056 #> Age                0.376  0.095   3.962  0.385  <.001 #>  #> Outcome: WellbeingScore #>  #> R-squared: 0.169    Adjusted R-squared: 0.134 #> Residual Standard Error: 10.622 #>  #> F-statistic: 4.814 on 4 and 95 DF, p-value: .001 #> Sum of Squares: #>   Regression: 2172.440 #>   Residual:   10717.560 #>   Total:      12890.000 #>   # To choose a non-default reference category: jdummy(community, Region, ref = "West") #> Dummy Variable Registration #>   Variable: Region (haven_labelled) #>   Reference category: Region_West #>   Dummy variables: Region_North, Region_South, Region_East #>   Cases: 100 (0 missing) #>  #> Note: this registration is stored for this session only. #> To keep it across sessions, save the data frame in R format (.rds): #>   jsave(community, "community.rds") #>  #> Next session, load that file to restore the registration: #>   community <- jload("community.rds") jlm(WellbeingScore ~ Region + Age) #> Linear Regression #> Using default data frame: community #>  #> Coefficients #>                     b      SE      t      β      p   #> ----------------  ------  -----  -----  -----  ----- #> (Intercept)       31.324  4.620  6.780         <.001 #> Region_North (1)   5.692  2.940  1.936          .056 #> Region_South (1)   5.322  3.290  1.618          .109 #> Region_East (1)    5.305  2.867  1.850          .067 #> Age                0.376  0.095  3.962  0.385  <.001 #>  #> Outcome: WellbeingScore #>  #> R-squared: 0.169    Adjusted R-squared: 0.134 #> Residual Standard Error: 10.622 #>  #> F-statistic: 4.814 on 4 and 95 DF, p-value: .001 #> Sum of Squares: #>   Regression: 2172.440 #>   Residual:   10717.560 #>   Total:      12890.000 #>   # FORCING NUMERIC TREATMENT # # Use numeric = ... when a variable has value labels (haven_labelled) # but you want it treated as a continuous score (e.g., a Likert # scale you want the slope-per-unit interpretation for). jlm(WellbeingScore ~ Age + Education, numeric = "Education") #> Linear Regression #> Using default data frame: community #>  #> Case Processing    Excluded  Remaining #>     Original              —        100 #>     Auto-listwise         6         94 #>     Analysis N            —         94 #>  #> Missing-data breakdown  From 100    % #>     Education #>       Missing              6      6.0 #>  #> ────────────────────────────────────── #>  #>  #> Coefficients #>                b      SE      t      β      p   #> -----------  ------  -----  -----  -----  ----- #> (Intercept)  27.970  3.860  7.245         <.001 #> Age           0.312  0.082  3.793  0.324  <.001 #> Education     3.700  0.689  5.373  0.459  <.001 #>  #> Outcome: WellbeingScore #>  #> R-squared: 0.339    Adjusted R-squared: 0.325 #> Residual Standard Error: 9.305 #>  #> F-statistic: 23.386 on 2 and 91 DF, p-value: <.001 #> Sum of Squares: #>   Regression: 4050.000 #>   Residual:   7879.829 #>   Total:      11929.830 #>   # Multiple overrides at once jlm(WellbeingScore ~ Education + Environment4 + Smoker,     numeric = c("Education", "Environment4"), categorical = "Smoker") #> Linear Regression #> Using default data frame: community #>  #> Case Processing    Excluded  Remaining #>     Original              —        100 #>     Auto-listwise        11         89 #>     Analysis N            —         89 #>  #> Missing-data breakdown  From 100    % #>     Education #>       Missing              6      6.0 #>     Smoker #>       Missing              5      5.0 #>  #> ────────────────────────────────────── #>  #>  #> Coefficients #>                   b      SE      t       β       p   #> --------------  ------  -----  ------  ------  ----- #> (Intercept)     41.893  3.729  11.234          <.001 #> Education        3.777  0.864   4.371   0.468  <.001 #> Environment4    -0.261  1.001  -0.261  -0.027   .795 #> Smoker_Yes (1)  -2.055  2.314  -0.888           .377 #>  #> Outcome: WellbeingScore #>  #> R-squared: 0.241    Adjusted R-squared: 0.215 #> Residual Standard Error: 10.189 #>  #> F-statistic: 9.016 on 3 and 85 DF, p-value: <.001 #> Sum of Squares: #>   Regression: 2807.688 #>   Residual:   8823.817 #>   Total:      11631.506 #>   # Not normally needed. You'd clear a default or registration only to # undo a mistake, or -- as in this example -- to reset state for testing. jdummy(community, NULL) #> Dummy registrations cleared for community: Region. juse(NULL) #> Default data frame cleared.

what: Load a data file into R — jload
title: Load a data file into R — jload
text: jload() reads data file assigns data frame environment. Supports SPSS (.sav), Stata (.dta), SAS (.sas7bdat, .xpt), Excel (.xlsx, .xls), CSV (.csv), R's native .rds format. file format determined entirely file extension — jload() reads extension (e.g. .sav, .dta, .xlsx) uses appropriate reader automatically. default, jload() looks file working directory. data folder configured joptions(data.dir = ...), folder searched first. full file path provided, used directly. data frame automatically named file (without extension). Use name argument specify different name.

title: Load a data file into R — jload
code: jload(   file,   name = NULL,   use = FALSE,   overwrite = FALSE,   package = FALSE,   check.missing = TRUE,   sheet = NULL,   preserve.udm = TRUE,   udm.notice = NULL,   quiet = FALSE )

title: Load a data file into R — jload
text: file Character string. filename (e.g. "mydata.sav") full file path (e.g. "C:/Projects/mydata.sav"). Use forward slashes file paths. extension omitted, jload() searches common data file types automatically. name Character string (optional). name assign data frame environment. omitted, name derived filename. use Logical. TRUE, automatically calls juse() loaded data frame set default jstats functions. Default FALSE. overwrite Logical. TRUE, overwrites existing object name without prompting. FALSE (default), prompts confirmation interactive sessions. non-interactive sessions, overwrites warning message. package Logical. TRUE, loads jstats example dataset shipped package (e.g. community, clinic) bare name, bypassing disk search. Use -named file working directory data folder otherwise shadow shipped dataset. FALSE (default), matching disk file takes precedence shipped dataset used file matches. file must bare name path extension package = TRUE. check.missing Logical. TRUE (default), scans numeric variables values look like coded missing values (e.g. -99, 999) reports . Set FALSE skip check. sheet Excel files . sheet read — either sheet name (character) sheet number (integer). Defaults first sheet. file multiple sheets sheet specified, message lists available sheets. preserve.udm Logical. TRUE (default), user-defined missing values arriving file preserved: SPSS-style codes -99 keep original numeric values data frame, metadata attached package's analysis functions still treat missing, Stata-style tagged values (., .b, ...) kept read. FALSE, forms converted plain NA import metadata stripped. Applies loaded file whose columns carry missing-value declarations — typically .sav, .dta, .sas7bdat files, .rds files saved data. .sav files, TRUE corresponds haven's user_na = TRUE. udm.notice Per-call override user-defined missing value (UDM) notification frequency. NULL (default) defers global setting joutput(). TRUE prints notification every load; FALSE suppresses ; NULL global level shows per session. See ?joutput full toggle behavior. quiet Logical; default FALSE. TRUE, suppresses jload()'s informational messages (directory-resolution note, file found, load summary, default-data note, UDM narrative, overriding udm.notice). Errors, warnings, multi-sheet advisory, overwrite prompt still shown.

title: Load a data file into R — jload
text: Invisibly returns loaded data frame. primary effect assigning data frame calling environment.

title: Load a data file into R — jload
text: File paths: Use forward slashes (/) file paths. copy path Windows File Explorer, replace backslashes forward slashes. R accept single backslashes file paths. File search order: path contains directory separator (/), path used directly. path bare filename, jload() checks: () folder named joptions("data.dir") set exists; (b) working directory. Auto-naming: data frame name derived filename stripping extension. resulting name starts digit (R allow variable name), must supply name argument. Excel files: Excel files (.xlsx, .xls) contain variable value labels. data loaded plain numeric, character, logical columns. Use jrelabel() add labels loading needed. Coded missing values: check.missing = TRUE, function scans numeric variables values appear coded missing values. whole-number values considered (coded missing values always integers like -99, 999, etc.). Two detection methods used: SPSS files, user-defined missing values stored file metadata reported high confidence. heuristic scan detects negative values among otherwise positive data extreme outlier values (5x range values). Detected values reported changed. Use jrecode convert NA needed.

title: Load a data file into R — jload
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # SPSS jload("community.sav") jload("community.sav", use = TRUE) jload("community.sav", name = "MySurvey")  # Stata jload("community.dta")  # SAS jload("community.sas7bdat") jload("community.xpt")  # Excel jload("community.xlsx") jload("community.xlsx", sheet = "Wave2") jload("community.xlsx", sheet = 2)  # CSV and R native jload("community.csv") jload("community.rds")  # Extension omitted -- jload searches for a matching file automatically jload("community")  # Full file path jload("C:/Projects/Data/community.dta")  # Quiet load (e.g. in a .Rprofile or startup script): suppresses the # informational messages while still loading. Errors and warnings still show. jload("community.rds", name = "MyData", quiet = TRUE) } # }

what: Logistic regression with SPSS-style output — jlogistic
title: Logistic regression with SPSS-style output — jlogistic
text: Fits binary logistic regression using stats::glm() family = binomial prints formatted output including omnibus model test, model summary statistics, coefficients table odds ratios (Exp(B)).

title: Logistic regression with SPSS-style output — jlogistic
code: jlogistic(   formula,   data,   subset = NULL,   variable.id = NULL,   numeric = NULL,   categorical = NULL,   count = NULL,   ci = NULL,   classification = FALSE,   diagnostics = NULL,   ref.categories = NULL,   full = FALSE,   case.processing.detail = NULL,   digits = NULL,   ...,   value.id = NULL )

title: Logistic regression with SPSS-style output — jlogistic
text: formula model formula, e.g. DV ~ IV1 + IV2. DV must binary variable coded 0/1. data data frame containing variables referenced formula. subset optional unquoted logical expression (e.g. Group == 1) subset cases call . variable.id Character NULL. Variable label display mode: one "", "names", "labels", "legend", "legend.bottom". "names" shows variable names ; "" shows "name: label"; "labels" replaces coefficient's variable name label Coefficients table (factor level decoration preserved) – best short labels; "legend" prints label legend just Coefficients table (coefficients/fit seam); "legend.bottom" prints end. NULL (default) defers joutput()'s variable.id setting. logical. numeric Optional character vector variable names treat continuous even value labels. categorical Optional character vector variable names treat categorical even lack value labels. count Optional character vector independent-variable names treat counts call (per-call counterpart jcount()). count predictor numeric-like, enters model exactly numeric ; argument provided symmetry analysis functions. binary dependent variable fixed, naming effect. variable listed count categorical. ci Logical NULL. TRUE, adds 95% confidence intervals Exp(B). NULL (default), defers joutput(). classification Logical. TRUE, prints classification table showing predicted vs observed outcomes. Default FALSE. diagnostics Logical, character vector, NULL. TRUE, prints VIF table. character vector, vif currently supported option. NULL (default), defers joutput(). ref.categories Logical NULL. Per-call override showing reference-categories block (baseline level dropped set dummy variables). NULL (default) defers joutput()'s ref.categories setting. Applies jlm() jlogistic() , since functions produce dummy-coded coefficient tables. full Logical. TRUE, turns ci, classification, diagnostics. override explicit FALSE values. case.processing.detail Per-call override Case Processing Summary detail tier: one "none", "totals", "per_code". NULL (default) uses active joutput() level default. digits Integer NULL. Number decimal places continuous statistics output tables (range 0-7; digits = 0 prints whole numbers trailing decimal point). affect p-values, percentages, integer quantities (counts, N, degrees freedom), keep fixed conventions. NULL (default) defers joutput()'s digits setting (default 3). ... Reserved argument-name checking. Passing , plots, show produce helpful error suggesting diagnostics instead. value.id Character NULL. Value-label display mode dummy category rows Coefficients table: one "" ("code: label", degrading bare code code label), "values" (bare code), "labels" (value label, degrading bare code none exists). reference category folded grouped variable's header follows mode. "legend" "legend.bottom" supported : coefficient table already pairs value label row, separate legend block duplicate . Passing either explicitly error; joutput() default "legend" "legend.bottom" tolerated rendered "", break bare call. Variables value labels render identically supported modes. NULL (default) defers joutput()'s value.id setting. Applies multi-category dummy predictors; continuous single-contrast (dichotomous) predictors unaffected. logical.

title: Logistic regression with SPSS-style output — jlogistic
text: Invisibly returns list class jst_logistic containing: model fitted glm object. model_type Character string logistic. model_frame model frame used fit model. formula_used formula dummy expansion. coefficients Formatted coefficient table (data frame). coefficients_raw Flat data frame raw, full-precision coefficient statistics (one row per coefficient): term (machine key, shared jlm), b, SE, Wald, df, p, exp_b, exp_ci_lower / exp_ci_upper odds-ratio CI bounds (present regardless ci display toggle). Carries outcome attribute. fit_raw List raw, full-precision model-level fit statistics: ll_model, ll_null, deviance, null_deviance, omnibus likelihood-ratio test (chi_sq, omnibus_df, omnibus_p), Cox & Snell Nagelkerke pseudo R-squared (cox_snell_r2, nagelkerke_r2), aic, n. nagelkerke_r2 Nagelkerke pseudo R-squared. cox_snell_r2 Cox & Snell pseudo R-squared. neg2ll -2 Log Likelihood. aic Akaike Information Criterion. omnibus Named vector: chi_square, df, p. n Number observations. predicts Character string describing model predicts. dummy_coef_names Names dummy variable columns. ref_cats Reference category descriptions. vif Named numeric vector VIF values, NULL. sample_info Pipeline missing data counts.

title: Logistic regression with SPSS-style output — jlogistic
text: dependent variable must coded 0/1. , function stops clear error message suggests appropriate jrecode() command. Handles haven-labelled variables, registered dummy variables via jdummy(), numeric/categorical overrides way jlm().

title: Logistic regression with SPSS-style output — jlogistic
code: # With explicit data frame -- Volunteer is already coded 0/1 jlogistic(Volunteer ~ Income + Age, data = community) #> Logistic Regression #>  #> Case Processing    Excluded  Remaining #>     Original              —        100 #>     Auto-listwise         6         94 #>     Analysis N            —         94 #>  #> Missing-data breakdown  From 100    % #>     Income #>       Missing              6      6.0 #>  #> ────────────────────────────────────── #>  #>  #> Coefficients #>                b      SE    Wald   df   p    Exp(B) #> -----------  ------  -----  -----  --  ----  ------ #> (Intercept)  -2.543  0.925  7.565   1  .006   0.079 #> Income        0.000  0.000  4.849   1  .028   1.000 #> Age           0.023  0.020  1.351   1  .245   1.023 #>  #> Outcome: Volunteer #>  #> Omnibus Test of Model Coefficients #> Chi-Square  df  p    #> ----------  --  ---- #>      8.548   2  .014 #>  #> Model Summary #> -2 Log Likelihood  Cox & Snell R²  Nagelkerke R²      AIC #> -----------------  --------------  -------------  ------- #>           119.027           0.087          0.117  125.027 #>  #> Dependent Variable Encoding #>   Modeled (1):   Yes #>   Reference (0): No  # A 1/2-coded dichotomy (Yes = 1, No = 2) must be recoded to 0/1 first df <- community df$OwnsHome01 <- jrecode(df, OwnsHome,                          map = "1=1; 2=0", labels = "0=No; 1=Yes") #>  #> Note: jrecode() returns the recoded values; assign them to a column to keep them: #>   df$<name> <- jrecode(...) #> To check the recode landed correctly, compare jfreq() on the original and the new column. jlogistic(OwnsHome01 ~ Income + Age, data = df) #> Logistic Regression #>  #> Case Processing    Excluded  Remaining #>     Original              —        100 #>     Auto-listwise         6         94 #>     Analysis N            —         94 #>  #> Missing-data breakdown  From 100    % #>     Income #>       Missing              6      6.0 #>  #> ────────────────────────────────────── #>  #>  #> Coefficients #>                b      SE     Wald   df    p    Exp(B) #> -----------  ------  -----  ------  --  -----  ------ #> (Intercept)  -5.586  1.268  19.421   1  <.001   0.004 #> Income        0.000  0.000   9.186   1   .002   1.000 #> Age           0.082  0.025  10.948   1  <.001   1.086 #>  #> Outcome: OwnsHome01 #>  #> Omnibus Test of Model Coefficients #> Chi-Square  df  p     #> ----------  --  ----- #>       30.4   2  <.001 #>  #> Model Summary #> -2 Log Likelihood  Cox & Snell R²  Nagelkerke R²     AIC #> -----------------  --------------  -------------  ------ #>             99.23           0.276          0.369  105.23 #>  #> Dependent Variable Encoding #>   Modeled (1):   Yes #>   Reference (0): No  # Using juse() default juse(community) #> Default data frame set to: community jlogistic(Volunteer ~ Income + Age) #> Logistic Regression #> Using default data frame: community #>  #> Case Processing    Excluded  Remaining #>     Original              —        100 #>     Auto-listwise         6         94 #>     Analysis N            —         94 #>  #> Missing-data breakdown  From 100    % #>     Income #>       Missing              6      6.0 #>  #> ────────────────────────────────────── #>  #>  #> Coefficients #>                b      SE    Wald   df   p    Exp(B) #> -----------  ------  -----  -----  --  ----  ------ #> (Intercept)  -2.543  0.925  7.565   1  .006   0.079 #> Income        0.000  0.000  4.849   1  .028   1.000 #> Age           0.023  0.020  1.351   1  .245   1.023 #>  #> Outcome: Volunteer #>  #> Omnibus Test of Model Coefficients #> Chi-Square  df  p    #> ----------  --  ---- #>      8.548   2  .014 #>  #> Model Summary #> -2 Log Likelihood  Cox & Snell R²  Nagelkerke R²      AIC #> -----------------  --------------  -------------  ------- #>           119.027           0.087          0.117  125.027 #>  #> Dependent Variable Encoding #>   Modeled (1):   Yes #>   Reference (0): No  # CATEGORICAL PREDICTORS # # Per-call: categorical = ... applies for one call only and does not # persist. jlogistic(Volunteer ~ Region + Age, categorical = "Region") #> Logistic Regression #> Using default data frame: community #>  #> Coefficients #>                     b      SE    Wald   df   p    Exp(B) #> ----------------  ------  -----  -----  --  ----  ------ #> (Intercept)       -2.144  0.901  5.662   1  .017   0.117 #> Region_South (1)   0.155  0.642  0.058   1  .809   1.168 #> Region_East (1)    0.692  0.570  1.474   1  .225   1.998 #> Region_West (1)   -0.396  0.596  0.441   1  .507   0.673 #> Age                0.041  0.020  4.474   1  .034   1.042 #>  #> Outcome: Volunteer #>  #> Omnibus Test of Model Coefficients #> Chi-Square  df  p    #> ----------  --  ---- #>      8.544   4  .074 #>  #> Model Summary #> -2 Log Likelihood  Cox & Snell R²  Nagelkerke R²      AIC #> -----------------  --------------  -------------  ------- #>           127.515           0.082           0.11  137.515 #>  #> Dependent Variable Encoding #>   Modeled (1):   Yes #>   Reference (0): No  # The recommended approach for repeated analyses: register the variable # with jdummy() before running jlogistic(). This sets categorical # treatment persistently across subsequent analyses. jdummy(community, Region) #> Dummy Variable Registration #>   Variable: Region (haven_labelled) #>   Reference category: Region_North #>   Dummy variables: Region_South, Region_East, Region_West #>   Cases: 100 (0 missing) #>  #> Note: this registration is stored for this session only. #> To keep it across sessions, save the data frame in R format (.rds): #>   jsave(community, "community.rds") #>  #> Next session, load that file to restore the registration: #>   community <- jload("community.rds") jlogistic(Volunteer ~ Region + Age) #> Logistic Regression #> Using default data frame: community #>  #> Coefficients #>                     b      SE    Wald   df   p    Exp(B) #> ----------------  ------  -----  -----  --  ----  ------ #> (Intercept)       -2.144  0.901  5.662   1  .017   0.117 #> Region_South (1)   0.155  0.642  0.058   1  .809   1.168 #> Region_East (1)    0.692  0.570  1.474   1  .225   1.998 #> Region_West (1)   -0.396  0.596  0.441   1  .507   0.673 #> Age                0.041  0.020  4.474   1  .034   1.042 #>  #> Outcome: Volunteer #>  #> Omnibus Test of Model Coefficients #> Chi-Square  df  p    #> ----------  --  ---- #>      8.544   4  .074 #>  #> Model Summary #> -2 Log Likelihood  Cox & Snell R²  Nagelkerke R²      AIC #> -----------------  --------------  -------------  ------- #>           127.515           0.082           0.11  137.515 #>  #> Dependent Variable Encoding #>   Modeled (1):   Yes #>   Reference (0): No  # To choose a non-default reference category: jdummy(community, Region, ref = "West") #> Dummy Variable Registration #>   Variable: Region (haven_labelled) #>   Reference category: Region_West #>   Dummy variables: Region_North, Region_South, Region_East #>   Cases: 100 (0 missing) #>  #> Note: this registration is stored for this session only. #> To keep it across sessions, save the data frame in R format (.rds): #>   jsave(community, "community.rds") #>  #> Next session, load that file to restore the registration: #>   community <- jload("community.rds") jlogistic(Volunteer ~ Region + Age) #> Logistic Regression #> Using default data frame: community #>  #> Coefficients #>                     b      SE    Wald   df   p    Exp(B) #> ----------------  ------  -----  -----  --  ----  ------ #> (Intercept)       -2.540  0.984  6.656   1  .010   0.079 #> Region_North (1)   0.396  0.596  0.441   1  .507   1.485 #> Region_South (1)   0.551  0.669  0.677   1  .410   1.734 #> Region_East (1)    1.088  0.581  3.504   1  .061   2.967 #> Age                0.041  0.020  4.474   1  .034   1.042 #>  #> Outcome: Volunteer #>  #> Omnibus Test of Model Coefficients #> Chi-Square  df  p    #> ----------  --  ---- #>      8.544   4  .074 #>  #> Model Summary #> -2 Log Likelihood  Cox & Snell R²  Nagelkerke R²      AIC #> -----------------  --------------  -------------  ------- #>           127.515           0.082           0.11  137.515 #>  #> Dependent Variable Encoding #>   Modeled (1):   Yes #>   Reference (0): No  # FORCING NUMERIC TREATMENT # # Use numeric = ... when a labelled variable should enter as a score. jlogistic(Volunteer ~ Age + Education, numeric = "Education") #> Logistic Regression #> Using default data frame: community #>  #> Case Processing    Excluded  Remaining #>     Original              —        100 #>     Auto-listwise         6         94 #>     Analysis N            —         94 #>  #> Missing-data breakdown  From 100    % #>     Education #>       Missing              6      6.0 #>  #> ────────────────────────────────────── #>  #>  #> Coefficients #>                b      SE     Wald   df    p    Exp(B) #> -----------  ------  -----  ------  --  -----  ------ #> (Intercept)  -4.484  1.209  13.755   1  <.001   0.011 #> Age           0.042  0.022   3.654   1   .056   1.043 #> Education     0.841  0.197  18.137   1  <.001   2.318 #>  #> Outcome: Volunteer #>  #> Omnibus Test of Model Coefficients #> Chi-Square  df  p     #> ----------  --  ----- #>     27.586   2  <.001 #>  #> Model Summary #> -2 Log Likelihood  Cox & Snell R²  Nagelkerke R²      AIC #> -----------------  --------------  -------------  ------- #>           100.633           0.254          0.342  106.633 #>  #> Dependent Variable Encoding #>   Modeled (1):   Yes #>   Reference (0): No  # Not normally needed. You'd clear a default or registration only to # undo a mistake, or -- as in this example -- to reset state for testing. jdummy(community, NULL) #> Dummy registrations cleared for community: Region. juse(NULL) #> Default data frame cleared.

what: Register variables as numeric for analysis — jnumeric
title: Register variables as numeric for analysis — jnumeric
text: jnumeric() tells jstats treat one variables numeric (continuous) wherever analysis class matters, overriding package's automatic structural guess. counterpart jdummy (categorical) jcount (count): variable carries exactly one registered intent time, registering numeric clears prior dummy count registration. Registration changes data assigns nothing – write df <- jnumeric(...). stored session, keyed data frame's name; save data frame R format (.rds) keep across sessions.

title: Register variables as numeric for analysis — jnumeric
Register variables as numeric for analysis — jnumeric
text: data data frame, omitted use juse default. jnumeric(NULL) clears numeric registrations juse default frame (, default set, frame carries ; several , asks rather wiping ). jnumeric(data, NULL) clears one frame's numeric registrations. Called arguments, jnumeric() lists session's numeric count registrations. ... One unquoted variable names register. remove Logical; TRUE, remove numeric registration named variables instead adding . clear.Logical; TRUE, clear numeric registrations every data frame carries .

title: Register variables as numeric for analysis — jnumeric
text: invisible(NULL). Called side effect session registration notebook.

title: Register variables as numeric for analysis — jnumeric
text: typical use small-range whole number structural classifier treat categorical (e.g. 0-6 attitude item) want analyzed continuous score.

title: Register variables as numeric for analysis — jnumeric
code: # Treat a labelled Likert item as a continuous score (slope-per-unit) jnumeric(community, Environment2)             # one labelled 1-5 item #> Numeric registration set for 'Environment2' in community. #> Note: this registration is stored for this session only. #> To keep it across sessions, save the data frame in R format (.rds): #>   jsave(community, "community.rds") #>  #> Next session, load that file to restore the registration: #>   community <- jload("community.rds") jnumeric(community, Environment2, Environment4)  # several at once #> Numeric registration set for 'Environment2', 'Environment4' in community. #> Note: registrations are stored for this session only. #> To keep them across sessions, save the data frame in R format (.rds): #>   jsave(community, "community.rds") #>  #> Next session, load that file to restore the registrations: #>   community <- jload("community.rds") jnumeric(community, Environment2, remove = TRUE) # unregister one #> Numeric registration removed for 'Environment2' in community. jnumeric()                          # list all registrations #> Variable Registrations #> Data frame: community #>  #>   Environment4: numeric #>  jnumeric(community, NULL)           # clear community's numeric registrations #> Numeric registrations cleared for community: Environment4. jnumeric(clear.all = TRUE)          # clear every frame's numeric registrations #> No numeric registrations to clear.

what: Set or display session-level package options — joptions
title: Set or display session-level package options — joptions
text: Controls session-wide settings affect package handles missing-value information related conventions. joptions complements joutput: joutput governs output verbosity tiering, joptions holds session-wide conventions plus small number per-function display defaults (currently jcorr() cell layout). Settings read fresh function call: changing setting data loaded retroactively transform data already memory. jconvert explicit transform path data already workspace.

title: Set or display session-level package options — joptions
code: joptions(   missing.convention = NULL,   udm.convention.codes = NULL,   data.dir = NULL,   corr.layout = NULL,   quiet = FALSE )

title: Set or display session-level package options — joptions
text: missing.convention One "none", "spss", "stata". See Slots. udm.convention.codes Numeric vector, length 1 3. See Slots. data.dir Character string (length 1), NULL. See Slots. corr.layout One "wide" "stacked", NULL. See Slots. quiet Logical; default FALSE. TRUE, joptions() applies change silently, suppressing status panel convention nudge. bare joptions() status query always prints regardless quiet.

title: Set or display session-level package options — joptions
text: Invisibly returns NULL. Called side effect updating session options printing status panel.

title: Set or display session-level package options — joptions
text: missing.convention Character, length 1. One "none", "spss", "stata". Default: "none". "none" preserves loaded data -(automatic conversion user-defined missing value (UDM) representations load time). "spss" "stata" opts load-time auto-conversion via jload, also supplies target convention fresh UDM declarations columns existing convention. udm.convention.codes Numeric vector, length 1 3, whole numbers, duplicates. Sign unconstrained. Default: c(-99, -98, -97). recommended UDM code set used jconvert translating Stata-style missing values (., .b, .c, .d) SPSS-form numeric codes, load-time diagnostic convention-matched detection. data.dir Character string (length 1), NULL. Default: NULL. NULL, jsave writes bare-filename saves working directory jload searches working directory. set, names folder (relative working directory) used save target bare-filename saves first directory searched bare-filename loads. folder auto-created first save already exist (nested paths created full). clear previously-set folder back default, pass data.dir = "" (empty string); passing data.dir = NULL leaves current setting unchanged (see Call patterns). Filenames containing directory separator (/) bypass setting taken literally. corr.layout Character, length 1. One "wide" "stacked". Default: "wide". default cell layout jcorr three variables correlated: "wide" puts r p one line N beneath; "stacked" stacks r, p, N three lines narrower table fits variables. per-call layout argument jcorr() overrides . lives rather joutput specific one function's output, tiered analysis-content toggle.

title: Set or display session-level package options — joptions
text: joptions() Print current settings panel. joptions(NULL) Reset slots defaults, print panel. joptions(slot = value, ...) Set one slots, print panel. Passing slot = NULL named argument leaves slot current value – useful setting one slot without touching another. reset single slot default, pass default value explicitly (e.g. joptions(missing.convention = "none")). data.dir's default NULL – already means "leave alone" – cleared instead data.dir = "".

title: Set or display session-level package options — joptions
text: Setting missing.convention "spss" "stata" triggers one-time scan globalenv() data frames whose predominant UDM convention differs newly-set value. mismatches exist, one-line notice lists affected data frames suggests jconvert alignment. notice informational; nothing changed. Plain data frames UDM-bearing columns – including course datasets standard form – trigger notice.

title: Set or display session-level package options — joptions
code: joptions()                                        # show current settings #> Options Settings #> User-defined missing values (UDMs) convention: None selected #> UDM convention codes: -99, -98, -97 #> Data folder: Working directory #> Correlation layout: wide #>  joptions(missing.convention = "spss")             # set, panel, nudge #> Options Settings #> User-defined missing values (UDMs) convention: SPSS #> UDM convention codes: -99, -98, -97 #> Data folder: Working directory #> Correlation layout: wide #>  joptions(udm.convention.codes = c(-99, -98))      # set, panel, no nudge #> Options Settings #> User-defined missing values (UDMs) convention: SPSS #> UDM convention codes: -99, -98 #> Data folder: Working directory #> Correlation layout: wide #>  joptions(data.dir = "Data")                       # set save/load folder #> Options Settings #> User-defined missing values (UDMs) convention: SPSS #> UDM convention codes: -99, -98 #> Data folder: Data (will be created on first save) #> Correlation layout: wide #>  joptions(missing.convention = "stata",          udm.convention.codes = c(-99, -98, -97)) # set both #> Options Settings #> User-defined missing values (UDMs) convention: Stata #> UDM convention codes: -99, -98, -97 #> Data folder: Data (will be created on first save) #> Correlation layout: wide #>  joptions(missing.convention = "spss",          udm.convention.codes = NULL)             # set mc, leave codes #> Options Settings #> User-defined missing values (UDMs) convention: SPSS #> UDM convention codes: -99, -98, -97 #> Data folder: Data (will be created on first save) #> Correlation layout: wide #>  joptions(NULL)                                    # reset all to defaults #> Options Settings #> User-defined missing values (UDMs) convention: None selected #> UDM convention codes: -99, -98, -97 #> Data folder: Working directory #> Correlation layout: wide #>

title: Set session-level output verbosity — joutput
text: Controls analysis functions display default. Three preset levels available, individual toggles can override specific settings within level. Per-call arguments analysis functions always take precedence joutput() settings.

title: Set session-level output verbosity — joutput
code: joutput(   level,   effect.size = NULL,   regression.ci = NULL,   means.ci = NULL,   levene = NULL,   posthoc = NULL,   diagnostics = NULL,   case.processing = NULL,   case.processing.detail = NULL,   variable.id = NULL,   value.id = NULL,   ref.categories = NULL,   udm.notice = NULL,   digits = NULL,   quiet = FALSE )

title: Set session-level output verbosity — joutput
text: level Character. One minimal, standard (default), full. omitted, prints current settings. NULL, resets defaults (standard toggle overrides). minimal Stripped-output power users. Core results – Case Processing Summary, variable labels, reference categories, effect sizes, CIs. standard Default. Suitable teaching routine use. Includes Case Processing Summary, reference categories, effect sizes, confidence intervals means mean differences (jt, jaov); regression coefficient CIs (jlm, jlogistic) reserved full. Variable labels default (variable.id = "names"); request label legend -table labels per call via variable.id toggle. full Everything standard plus variable label legend (variable.id = "legend"), regression coefficient confidence intervals, assumption checks (Levene's test), post-hoc tests, regression diagnostics, detailed Case Processing Summary (per-code missing breakdown). effect.size Logical NULL. Override level's default effect size display. regression.ci Logical NULL. Override level's default confidence intervals regression coefficients (jlm, jlogistic). minimal standard, full. means.ci Logical NULL. Override level's default confidence intervals means mean differences (jt, jaov). minimal, standard full. levene Logical NULL. Override level's default Levene's test display. posthoc Logical NULL. Override level's default post-hoc test display (jaov ). diagnostics Logical NULL. Override level's default regression diagnostic output (jlm ). case.processing Three-state toggle. TRUE forces Case Processing Summary print every call. FALSE suppresses every call. NULL (auto-suppress default standard tier) prints call something report – pipeline state active (jsubset, jcomplete, per-call subset), listwise deletion excluded least one case (listwise functions like jlm, jt), per-variable discrepancy notification fires (jdesc/jfreq). minimal tier sets FALSE; full tier sets TRUE; standard tier sets NULL. case.processing.detail Detail tier Case Processing Summary's missing-data breakdown: "none" (bottom table), "totals" (one summed missing row per variable), "per_code" (per user-defined missing value code plus system-missing). minimal tier defaults "none", standard "totals", full "per_code". variable.id Character NULL. Variable label display mode, one "", "names", "labels", "legend", "legend.bottom". "names" shows variable names ; "" shows "name: label", labels block. "labels" replaces variable names labels analysis output (table rows, captions, crosstab dimnames, jplot axis/legend titles) – best labels short. "legend" keeps names place prints label legend function's mid position (jlm/jlogistic coefficients fit blocks; jfreq variable's table; elsewhere directly single table). "legend.bottom" keeps names place prints one consolidated legend end output. minimal standard tiers default "none"; full tier defaults "legend". logical – TRUE/FALSE accepted. value.id Character NULL. Value-label display mode categorical levels appear jfreq valid rows, jt/jaov group descriptives, jcrosstab axes, grouped jdesc headers. One "" ("code: label", degrading bare code code label), "values" (bare stored code), "labels" (value label, degrading bare code per code none exists). "legend" "legend.bottom" keep bare code table print value-label legend ("legend" per-table, "legend.bottom" consolidated multiple tables produced). Variables value labels render identically three modes, -op plain numeric data. minimal tier defaults "values"; standard full tiers default "". Distinct variable.id, governs one-per-variable descriptive label. logical. ref.categories Logical NULL. Override level's default reference categories block (registered dummies). udm.notice Three-state toggle controlling user-defined missing-value (UDM) notification emitted jload() .sav files. TRUE prints notification every load involves UDM-bearing variables; FALSE suppresses entirely; NULL ("auto") prints first time session, suppresses . Standard level uses NULL (auto), minimal uses FALSE, full uses TRUE. digits Integer NULL. Number decimal places shown continuous statistics analysis-function output tables (range 0-7; digits = 0 prints whole numbers trailing decimal point). affect p-values, percentages, integer quantities (counts, N, degrees freedom), keep fixed conventions. three preset levels default 3. quiet Logical; default FALSE. TRUE, joutput() applies level/toggle change silently (status panel printed). bare joutput() status query always prints regardless quiet.

title: Set session-level output verbosity — joutput
text: Invisibly returns NULL. Called side effect setting session options.

title: Set session-level output verbosity — joutput
code: joutput("standard")                       # effect sizes + means/diff CIs (jt, jaov) #> Output Settings #> Level: standard #>   effect.size: ON #>   regression.ci: OFF #>   means.ci: ON #>   levene: OFF #>   posthoc: OFF #>   diagnostics: OFF #>   case.processing: AUTO #>   case.processing.detail: TOTALS #>   variable.id: NAMES #>   value.id: BOTH #>   ref.categories: ON #>   udm.notice: AUTO #>   digits: 3 #>  joutput("standard", regression.ci = TRUE) # also show jlm/jlogistic coefficient CIs #> Output Settings #> Level: standard #>   effect.size: ON #>   regression.ci: ON (override) #>   means.ci: ON #>   levene: OFF #>   posthoc: OFF #>   diagnostics: OFF #>   case.processing: AUTO #>   case.processing.detail: TOTALS #>   variable.id: NAMES #>   value.id: BOTH #>   ref.categories: ON #>   udm.notice: AUTO #>   digits: 3 #>  joutput("full")                         # everything #> Output Settings #> Level: full #>   effect.size: ON #>   regression.ci: ON #>   means.ci: ON #>   levene: ON #>   posthoc: ON #>   diagnostics: ON #>   case.processing: ON #>   case.processing.detail: PER_CODE #>   variable.id: LEGEND #>   value.id: BOTH #>   ref.categories: ON #>   udm.notice: ON #>   digits: 3 #>  joutput()                               # show current settings #> Output Settings #> Level: full #>   effect.size: ON #>   regression.ci: ON #>   means.ci: ON #>   levene: ON #>   posthoc: ON #>   diagnostics: ON #>   case.processing: ON #>   case.processing.detail: PER_CODE #>   variable.id: LEGEND #>   value.id: BOTH #>   ref.categories: ON #>   udm.notice: ON #>   digits: 3 #>  joutput(NULL)                           # reset to defaults #> Output Settings #> Reset to defaults (standard, no toggle overrides). #>

what: Visualise jst_* result objects or plot variables directly from a data frame — jplot
title: Visualise jst_* result objects or plot variables directly from a data frame — jplot
text: Unified plotting function. Can called three ways:

title: Visualise jst_* result objects or plot variables directly from a data frame — jplot
code: jplot(x, which = "core", ...)  # Default S3 method jplot(   x,   ...,   by = NULL,   type = NULL,   line = FALSE,   equation = TRUE,   r2 = TRUE,   band = "ci",   subset = NULL,   labels = NULL,   numeric = NULL,   categorical = NULL,   count = NULL )  # S3 method for class 'jst_lm' jplot(   x,   which = "core",   focal = NULL,   at = "zero",   equation = TRUE,   r2 = TRUE,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'jst_logistic' jplot(x, which = "core", focal = NULL, at = "zero", ...)  # S3 method for class 'jst_ttest' jplot(x, which = "core", ...)  # S3 method for class 'jst_anova' jplot(x, which = "core", ...)  # S3 method for class 'jst_corr' jplot(x, which = "core", ...)  # S3 method for class 'jst_crosstab' jplot(x, which = "core", ...)  # S3 method for class 'jst_desc' jplot(x, which = "core", ...)  # S3 method for class 'jst_freq' jplot(x, which = "core", ...)

title: Visualise jst_* result objects or plot variables directly from a data frame — jplot
text: x result object one package's analysis functions (result-object form), data frame (data-first form). Character vector. core (default), , one specific plot names valid object's class. (Result-object form .) ... Additional arguments: result-object form passed class-specific methods; data-first form unquoted variable names (1 2). Unquoted variable name group-coloring (data-first form). type Character. Plot type override data-first form. One histogram, bar, scatter, box, grouped_bar. NULL (default), auto-detected variable types. line Controls line overlay data-first scatter plots. One FALSE (default; line), TRUE (alias lm), lm, loess, connect. equation Logical. TRUE (default), displays equation subtitle line = "lm" scatter plots (data-first form) jst_lm fit plots (result-object form). r2 Logical. TRUE (default), displays R-squared subtitle alongside equation. band Character. Uncertainty band type line = "lm" scatter plots. One ci (default; 95% confidence band mean, flares ends), pi (95% prediction interval individual observations), see (constant-width band +/- t*SEE; useful teaching homoskedasticity), none. subset Optional unquoted logical expression filter cases call (data-first form). labels Character NULL. Variable label display mode (data-first formula forms): one "", "names", "labels", "legend", "legend.bottom". "names" uses variable names axis/legend titles; "labels" uses variable's label axis/legend title instead (falling back name unlabelled) prints console legend; "legend" "legend.bottom" keep names axes print console label legend. "" accepted currently renders "names" plots ("name: label" form plot titles deferred later phase). NULL (default) defers joutput()'s variable.id setting. logical. numeric Optional character vector plotted-variable names treat continuous call (per-call counterpart jnumeric()). jplot() variable's class chooses geometry, forces numeric handling (histogram single variable; scatter / numeric axis formula two-variable forms). Applies plotted variables , grouping variable. categorical Optional character vector plotted-variable names treat categorical call (per-call counterpart jdummy() plotting purposes). Forces categorical geometry (bar single variable; box / categorical axis formula form). variable listed categorical numeric/count. count Optional character vector plotted-variable names treat counts call (per-call counterpart jcount()). count numeric-like plotting, draws numeric; provided symmetry analysis functions. focal Unquoted name independent variable place x-axis jst_lm / jst_logistic fit probability plots. Defaults first IV model. Character string named list specifying non-focal independent variables held drawing fitted line jst_lm / jst_logistic methods. One zero (default), mean, mixed (categorical 0, interval mean), named list list(Var1 = value, ...).

title: Visualise jst_* result objects or plot variables directly from a data frame — jplot
text: Invisibly, single ggplot object one plot produced, named list ggplot objects multiple produced (result-object form). Invisibly returns ggplot object data-first form.

title: Visualise jst_* result objects or plot variables directly from a data frame — jplot
text: Result-object form: Pass result object returned one package's analysis functions. Produces appropriate plots class result (see valid plot names ). Formula form (plots distinguish DV IV): Pass formula first argument, followed optionally data frame. Used scatterplots boxplots, consistent formula syntax jlm(), jaov(), jt(). DV left ~ goes y-axis; IV right goes x-axis. single-IV formulas supported ; multi-IV models, fit jlm() pass result jplot(). Variable-list form (distributions counts): Pass data frame followed one two unquoted variable names. Used histograms (1 numeric), bar charts (1 categorical), grouped bar charts (2 categorical). Calls otherwise auto-detect scatter boxplot produce helpful error directing formula form. Supports pipeline integration (jsubset, jcomplete, per-call subset), grouping via = , regression lines equation/R-squared/band annotations. Valid plot names class (result-object form): jst_lm: fit, predicted, effects, coef, vif, residuals, qq, scale, cooks, leverage jst_logistic: probability, roc, calibration, binned, cooks, leverage, coef, vif jst_ttest, jst_anova: box jst_corr: heatmap, scatter (scatter requires exactly 2 variables correlation) jst_crosstab: bar shortcut keyword core (default) produces curated default set class; produces every plot class supports. Valid plot types data-first form: histogram, bar, scatter, box, grouped_bar. Valid line values: FALSE (default), TRUE (alias lm), lm, loess, connect. Valid band values: ci (default confidence band around regression line, flares ends), pi (prediction interval individual observations, wider), see (constant-width +/- t*SEE band illustrating homoskedasticity assumption), none (band).

title: Visualise jst_* result objects or plot variables directly from a data frame — jplot
text: jplot(default): default method: scatter box plot formula (DV ~ IV), histogram bar chart data frame one variables. jplot(jst_lm): diagnostic, coefficient (forest), fitted-effect plots jlm() linear-regression result. jplot(jst_logistic): predicted-probability (S-curve) coefficient plots jlogistic() result. jplot(jst_ttest): group-comparison box plot jt() result, group means marked. jplot(jst_anova): group-comparison box plot jaov() result, group means marked. jplot(jst_corr): heat-map correlation matrix jcorr() result, scatter plot single pair. jplot(jst_crosstab): grouped bar chart cell counts jcrosstab() result. jplot(jst_desc): (planned) direct plotting jdesc() result yet available; method points data-first form, example jplot(data, Variable). jplot(jst_freq): (planned) direct plotting jfreq() result yet available; method points data-first form, example jplot(data, Variable).

title: Visualise jst_* result objects or plot variables directly from a data frame — jplot
code: # Result-object form m <- jlm(WellbeingScore ~ Income + Age, community) #> Linear Regression #>  #> Case Processing    Excluded  Remaining #>     Original              —        100 #>     Auto-listwise         6         94 #>     Analysis N            —         94 #>  #> Missing-data breakdown  From 100    % #>     Income #>       Missing              6      6.0 #>  #> ────────────────────────────────────── #>  #>  #> Coefficients #>                b      SE      t      β      p   #> -----------  ------  -----  -----  -----  ----- #> (Intercept)  29.287  3.610  8.113         <.001 #> Income        0.000  0.000  6.416  0.549  <.001 #> Age           0.170  0.083  2.060  0.176   .042 #>  #> Outcome: WellbeingScore #>  #> R-squared: 0.387    Adjusted R-squared: 0.373 #> Residual Standard Error: 8.925 #>  #> F-statistic: 28.707 on 2 and 91 DF, p-value: <.001 #> Sum of Squares: #>   Regression: 4573.217 #>   Residual:   7248.527 #>   Total:      11821.745 #>  jplot(m)                            # core diagnostics + fit plot     jplot(m, which = "coef")            # coefficient forest plot #> `height` was translated to `width`.  jplot(m, which = "fit", focal = Age, at = "mean")   # Formula form (scatter and box) jplot(WellbeingScore ~ Income, community)               # scatter #> Scatterplot: WellbeingScore and Income #>   jplot(WellbeingScore ~ Income, community, line = "lm")  # + regression line #> Scatterplot: WellbeingScore and Income #>   jplot(WellbeingScore ~ Income, community, line = "lm", band = "see") #> Scatterplot: WellbeingScore and Income #>   jplot(WellbeingScore ~ Income, community, by = Volunteer, line = "lm") #> Scatterplot: WellbeingScore and Income by Volunteer #>  #> Ignoring unknown labels: #> • fill : "Volunteer"   # Boxplot: assert the grouping variable as categorical (labelled # variables otherwise enter numerically; jdummy() registration also works) jplot(WellbeingScore ~ Region, community, categorical = "Region") #> Boxplot: WellbeingScore and Region #>    # Variable-list form (distributions and counts) jplot(community, Age)                      # histogram #> Histogram: Age #>   jplot(community, Region)                   # bar chart #> Bar Chart: Region #>   jplot(community, Region, Volunteer,        # grouped bar chart       categorical = c("Region", "Volunteer")) #> Grouped Bar Chart: Region and Volunteer #>    # Using juse() default (formula form; omit the data frame) juse(community) #> Default data frame set to: community jplot(WellbeingScore ~ Income)               # scatter #> Scatterplot: WellbeingScore and Income #> Using default data frame: community #>   jplot(WellbeingScore ~ Income, line = "lm")  # + regression line #> Scatterplot: WellbeingScore and Income #> Using default data frame: community #>

what: Recode a variable with explicit value mapping and optional labels — jrecode
title: Recode a variable with explicit value mapping and optional labels — jrecode
text: jrecode() recodes variable using simple map string specifies old values converted new values. designed situations need collapse categories, change numeric codes, recode dichotomies. Variable value labels handled automatically. Map labels rules can also produce missing values: plain system NA via NA / System / SYSMIS aliases, Stata-style tagged missing values (..z) active convention Stata. See Missing values map canonical patterns convention.

title: Recode a variable with explicit value mapping and optional labels — jrecode
code: jrecode(data, orig.var, map, labels = NULL, convention = NULL)

title: Recode a variable with explicit value mapping and optional labels — jrecode
text: data data frame containing original variable. orig.var variable recode (unquoted, e.g. AgeGroup). map quoted string specifying recode rules, using format "old=new" rules separated semicolons. Multiple old values mapping new value separated commas left side. optional else clause controls happens values covered map: else clause: function stops message values left unmapped, can fix map proceeding. else=NA (also else=System else=SYSMIS): unmapped values deliberately set system NA. else=copy: unmapped values carried across unchanged. else=.(Stata-style missing-value token, Stata convention ): unmapped values set Stata-style missing value. Individual values can also mapped system NA using aliases: "-5=NA", "-5=System", "-5=SYSMIS". Stata convention, values can mapped Stata-style missing-value tokens: "-99=.; -98=.b". Examples: "1=1; 2=0" "1=1; 2,3=2; 4,5=3; else=NA" "1=1; 2=0; else=copy" "-5=System; else=copy" "3=1; 4=2; else=." (Stata convention ) labels Optional. quoted string specifying value labels new variable, using format "code=Label Text" rules separated semicolons. supplied, labels used -. left side rule may numeric code , Stata convention, Stata-style missing-value token (..z). Tagged-NA labels stored tag , numeric code. omitted, function attempts transfer value labels automatically original variable. works original variable value labels mapping one--one (categories collapsed). categories collapsed, labels transferred automatically note printed. Example: "1=Male; 0=Female" ".=Refused; .b=know". convention Optional. One "spss", "stata", NULL (default). Controls whether Stata-style missing-value tokens (..z) accepted map labels arguments. Inert Stata-style missing-value tokens appear either argument. NULL, convention resolved joptions("missing.convention"); also unset, default SPSS. users set convention top session via joptions() (.Rprofile) rather supplying argument every call. See ?joptions details.

title: Recode a variable with explicit value mapping and optional labels — jrecode
text: haven_labelled vector recoded values, variable label, (supplied auto-transferred) value labels applied. Assign new column data frame: MyData$AgeGroupR <- jrecode(MyData, AgeGroup, map = "...")

title: Recode a variable with explicit value mapping and optional labels — jrecode
text: function accepts haven-labelled, plain numeric, factor variables. variable label original variable carried across automatically "(recoded)" appended. original variable variable label, variable name used instead. Value labels handled three ways, order priority: labels supplied, labels used -. labels omitted original variable value labels, automatically transferred new codes — provided mapping one--one (collapsing). example, recoding 1/2 1/0 carry "Yes" "" across new codes automatically. categories collapsed (multiple old values map one new value), automatic transfer possible note printed directing supply labels manually. NA values original variable always set NA new variable, regardless else setting. Values appear coded missing values (e.g. -99, -9, 999) SPSS another package automatically detected set NA, even else=copy used. note printed occurs. map include else clause unmapped values variable, function stops message listing unmapped values can fix map proceeding. map specifies values exist original variable, warning issued (function continues). helps catch typos map string. Missing values map. package supports two conventions representing user-defined missing values (UDMs), syntax producing UDMs jrecode() depends one active: SPSS convention (default), UDMs real numeric codes carrying metadata flags missing. two-step canonical pattern : jrecode() call assigns numeric sentinel -99; subsequent jdeclare_udm() call attaches label flags -99 missing. Labeling -99 inside labels argument unnecessary — jdeclare_udm() owns label. Stata convention, UDMs typed missing cells marked Stata-style tags (..z). single-call canonical pattern : Stata convention, jdeclare_udm() needed pattern — jrecode() handles value recoding Stata-style missing-value labeling one call. Writing Stata-style missing-value tokens active convention SPSS raises informative error echoes user's call rewritten SPSS-style syntax. Switching convention session-wide one line: joptions(missing.convention = "stata").
code: df$EducR <- jrecode(df, Education,                     map    = "1,2=1; 3=2; 4,5=3; -99,-98=-99",                     labels = "1=High school or less; 2=Some college; 3=Degree") df <- jdeclare_udm(df, EducR, codes = c(Refused = -99)) df$EducR <- jrecode(df, Education,                     map    = "1,2=1; 3=2; 4,5=3; else=.a",                     labels = "1=High school or less; 2=Some college; 3=Degree; .a=Refused")

title: Recode a variable with explicit value mapping and optional labels — jrecode
code: # Recode with explicit labels (a 1/2 dichotomy to 0/1) df <- community df$OwnsHome01 <- jrecode(df, OwnsHome,                          map    = "1=1; 2=0",                          labels = "0=No; 1=Yes") #>  #> Note: jrecode() returns the recoded values; assign them to a column to keep them: #>   df$<name> <- jrecode(...) #> To check the recode landed correctly, compare jfreq() on the original and the new column.  # Collapse categories (must supply labels) df$RegionR <- jrecode(df, Region,                       map    = "1,2=1; 3,4=2",                       labels = "1=North or South; 2=East or West") #>  #> Note: jrecode() returns the recoded values; assign them to a column to keep them: #>   df$<name> <- jrecode(...) #> To check the recode landed correctly, compare jfreq() on the original and the new column.  # Use else=copy to carry unspecified values across unchanged df$EducR <- jrecode(df, Education,                     map    = "5=4; else=copy",                     labels = "4=Bachelor's degree or higher") #> Note: -99 ("Refused"), -98 ("Don't know") are declared missing values and were kept on the recoded variable. #> To convert them to plain NA instead, map them to NA (for example -99=NA). #>  #> Note: jrecode() returns the recoded values; assign them to a column to keep them: #>   df$<name> <- jrecode(...) #> To check the recode landed correctly, compare jfreq() on the original and the new column.  # Use else=NA to deliberately drop unspecified values to system NA df$EducR2 <- jrecode(df, Education,                      map    = "4=1; 5=1; else=NA",                      labels = "1=College degree") #> Note: -99 ("Refused"), -98 ("Don't know") are declared missing values and were kept on the recoded variable. #> To convert them to plain NA instead, map them to NA (for example -99=NA). #>  #> Note: jrecode() returns the recoded values; assign them to a column to keep them: #>   df$<name> <- jrecode(...) #> To check the recode landed correctly, compare jfreq() on the original and the new column.  # Convert a specific coded missing value to system NA df$EducR3 <- jrecode(df, Education, map = "-99=System; else=copy") #> Note: -98 ("Don't know") is a declared missing value and was kept on the recoded variable. #> To convert it to a plain NA instead, add -98=NA to the map. #>  #> Note: jrecode() returns the recoded values; assign them to a column to keep them: #>   df$<name> <- jrecode(...) #> To check the recode landed correctly, compare jfreq() on the original and the new column.  # Stata convention: Stata-style missing-value tokens in map and labels # (single call; convention = "stata" scopes the choice to this call only) df$EducR4 <- jrecode(df, Education,                      map    = "1,2=1; 3,4,5=2; else=.a",                      labels = "1=No college; 2=College; .a=Refused",                      convention = "stata") #> Note: -99 ("Refused"), -98 ("Don't know") are declared missing values and were kept on the recoded variable. #> To convert them to plain NA instead, map them to NA (for example -99=NA). #>  #> Note: jrecode() returns the recoded values; assign them to a column to keep them: #>   df$<name> <- jrecode(...) #> To check the recode landed correctly, compare jfreq() on the original and the new column.  # Using juse() default juse(df) #> Default data frame set to: df df$RegionR2 <- jrecode(Region, map = "1,2=1; 3,4=2",                        labels = "1=North or South; 2=East or West") #>  #> Note: jrecode() returns the recoded values; assign them to a column to keep them: #>   df$<name> <- jrecode(...) #> To check the recode landed correctly, compare jfreq() on the original and the new column.

what: Apply variable and value labels to a variable — jrelabel
title: Apply variable and value labels to a variable — jrelabel
text: jrelabel() attaches variable label /value labels variable data frame. designed simple label applicator — recode values compare variables. Use add labels recode, fix missing labels, label variable needs . function accepts haven-labelled, plain numeric, factor, character variables. output always haven_labelled vector, compatible jstats functions. labels var.label arguments optional. neither supplied, function returns variable unchanged haven_labelled vector. variable already labels, silently overwritten new labels provided.

title: Apply variable and value labels to a variable — jrelabel
code: jrelabel(data, var, labels = NULL, var.label = NULL)

title: Apply variable and value labels to a variable — jrelabel
text: data data frame containing variable. var variable label (unquoted, e.g. StatusR). labels Optional. quoted string specifying value labels using format "code=Label Text" rules separated semicolons. Examples: "1=Yes; 0=" "1=Employed; 2=Unemployed; 3=Student; 4=Retired" var.label Optional. quoted string use variable label (description shown jdesc(), jfreq(), etc.). omitted, existing variable label preserved. variable existing label, variable label set.

title: Apply variable and value labels to a variable — jrelabel
text: haven_labelled vector requested labels applied. Assign back column data frame: MyData$VarName <- jrelabel(MyData, VarName, ...)

title: Apply variable and value labels to a variable — jrelabel
code: # Add value labels after a recode df <- data.frame(Status = c(1, 2, 1, 2, 1, 2)) df$StatusR <- ifelse(df$Status == 1, 1, 0) df$StatusR <- jrelabel(df, StatusR, labels = "1=Yes; 0=No",                        var.label = "Status (recoded)")  # Add just a variable label df$StatusR <- jrelabel(df, StatusR, var.label = "Employment Status")  # Add just value labels df$StatusR <- jrelabel(df, StatusR, labels = "1=Yes; 0=No")  # Using juse() default juse(df) #> Default data frame set to: df df$StatusR <- jrelabel(StatusR, labels = "1=Active; 0=Inactive")

what: Save a data frame to a file — jsave
title: Save a data frame to a file — jsave
text: jsave() writes data frame file. Supports SPSS (.sav), Stata (.dta), SAS interchange (.xpt), Excel (.xlsx), CSV (.csv), R's native .rds format. file format determined entirely file extension provide — example, "mydata.sav" saves SPSS, "mydata.dta" saves Stata, "mydata.xlsx" saves Excel. Changing extension changes format. default, jsave() writes bare-filename saves working directory, matching base R's saveRDS() write.csv(). save subfolder, set joptions(data.dir = "...") per session (.Rprofile). Filenames containing directory separator (/) bypass setting taken literally. data argument omitted, default data frame set juse() used.

title: Save a data frame to a file — jsave
code: jsave(data, file, overwrite = FALSE, preserve.udm = TRUE)

title: Save a data frame to a file — jsave
text: data data frame (unquoted). omitted, uses default set juse(). file Character string. filename extension (e.g. "mydata.sav") full file path. Use forward slashes file paths. overwrite Logical. TRUE, overwrites existing file without prompting. FALSE (default), prompts confirmation interactive sessions. non-interactive sessions, stops error. preserve.udm Logical. TRUE (default), missing-value declarations written stand; formats store (notably Excel CSV) drop metadata, SPSS-style codes -99 read back ordinary numbers. FALSE, codes blanked plain NA writing, become empty cells. Mirrors preserve.udm argument jload. pre-flight checks .sav, .dta, .xpt formats run step, missing-value form target format represent still reported blocked rather silently dropped.

title: Save a data frame to a file — jsave
text: Invisibly returns NULL. Called side effect writing file disk.

title: Save a data frame to a file — jsave
text: File paths: Use forward slashes (/) file paths. copy path Windows File Explorer, replace backslashes forward slashes. R accept single backslashes file paths. File location: path contains directory separator, file saved exact location. path bare filename joptions("data.dir") set, file saved folder (auto-created yet exist). path bare filename joptions("data.dir") unset (default), file saved working directory. Format notes: SPSS (.sav) Stata (.dta) preserve variable labels value labels. Excel (.xlsx) CSV (.csv) preserve variable value labels. R native (.rds) preserves data frame exactly exists R, including attributes. Stata files written version 14 format. Legacy Excel format (.xls) supported saving. Use .xlsx instead.

title: Save a data frame to a file — jsave
code: # A runnable save into R's session temporary folder jsave(community, file.path(tempdir(), "community.sav"), overwrite = TRUE) #> Saved community to /tmp/RtmpcdlOkI/community.sav (SPSS format; 100 cases, 15 variables)  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # The file extension determines the format --- # the same data frame can be saved in any supported format jsave(community, "community.sav")         # SPSS jsave(community, "community.xlsx")        # Excel jsave(community, "community.csv")         # CSV jsave(community, "community.rds")         # R native  # Stata and SAS formats cannot carry community's SPSS-form missing-value # declarations -- convert first (jsave() pre-flights this and says so) jsave(jconvert(community, to = "stata"), "community.dta")   # Stata jsave(jconvert(community, to = "baseR"), "community.xpt")   # SAS interchange  # Using juse() default jsave(, "community.sav")  # Full file path jsave(community, "C:/Output/community.sav") } # }

title: Data screening overview — jscreen
text: Provides quick overview data frame screening. red "Data Screening" title printed first, short header block (case variable counts, cases missing data, variables outliers), followed three tables: Variable Types table (Base R storage type, jstats analysis-role class, optional sub-class, optional classification source, distinct-value counts, optional central- tendency columns), Missing Data & Outliers table, – variable labels shown – Variable Labels table last. Handles haven-labelled date/time variables gracefully.

title: Data screening overview — jscreen
code: jscreen(   data,   ...,   outlier.sd = 3,   subset = NULL,   variable.id = NULL,   value.id = NULL,   types = TRUE,   issues = TRUE,   r.type = FALSE,   stats = FALSE,   digits = NULL )

title: Data screening overview — jscreen
text: data data frame. ... Optional unquoted variable names screen. omitted, variables data frame screened. outlier.sd Numeric. Number standard deviations mean flag potential outliers (Numeric-class variables ). Default 3. subset optional unquoted logical expression (e.g. Group == 1) subset cases call . Applied jcomplete jsubset. affect function calls. variable.id Character NULL. Variable label display mode: one "", "names", "labels", "legend", "legend.bottom". "names" shows variable names ; "" shows "name: label"; "labels" shows labels table's Variable column (best short labels); "legend" "legend.bottom" keep names print label legend tables. NULL (default) defers joutput()'s variable.id setting. logical. value.id supported jscreen(). function display value labels, passing argument error. exists return clear message rather misreporting token missing variable. Leave NULL (default). types Logical. TRUE (default), prints Variable Types table. Set FALSE suppress . issues Logical. TRUE (default), prints Missing Data & Outliers table, lists variables actually missing data flagged outliers (clean variables omitted). Set FALSE suppress table entirely. Suppressing types, issues, labels together leaves header block. r.type Logical. TRUE, adds "Base R Type" column (numeric / haven_labelled / factor / character / date-time) Variable Types table, showing variable's storage type alongside jstats class. Default FALSE: storage type expert detail (main signal "variable carries value labels / came SPSS Stata"), opt-rather shown default. returned data frame always includes regardless setting. stats Logical character. Adds central-tendency columns Variable Types table numeric-like variables. FALSE (default) shows none; TRUE shows Mean Median; "mean" "median" shows one. Numeric Count variables show ; numeric dichotomy shows raw mean blank median; N-category non-numeric variables blank. returned data frame always includes Mean Median regardless setting. digits Integer NULL. Number decimal places Mean Median columns. NULL (default) defers joutput()'s digits setting (default 3).

title: Data screening overview — jscreen
